races.race_name: Revamp Conservation And Restoration Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Sun Goddess
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly cost 120,000GBP as a yearling; forced a dead heat for second on debut at Naas (good) when finishing powerfully over 5f, just a head behind better fancied stablemate; this step up in trip is likely to suit and she looks like a big player.
runners.comment: Forced a dead-heat second on debut when finishing powerfully over 5f; big player

runners.name: Lan De Ghaire
runners.spotlight: Beaten 1l over C&D on debut (good), showing signs of greenness before running on well for third; led inside the final furlong at Roscommon over 7.5f (good) before fading into a 0.75l third; in the mix again.
runners.comment: Beaten by 1l or less in both maiden starts; in the mix again

runners.name: Amsterdam's Law
runners.spotlight: 150,000USD yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to US winners Dream Unified (5f 2yo-1m dirt) and Brie's Mission (1m turf Flat/2m1f hurdle); dam US 8.5f winner, out of 6f 2yo winning half-sister to US Grade 1 winners JB's Thunder (8.5f) and Carpe Diem (8.5f/1m1f); trainer is on a hot streak with juveniles.
runners.comment: 150,000USD yearling; half-sister to two winners; trainer is on a hot streak with juveniles

runners.name: Akari
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly; 100,000euros yearling; third foal; half-sister to Italian 8.7f 2yo winner Ancient Story; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 75), sister to 1m4f/13.5f winner Moon Daisy, half-sister to 7f 2yo Listed winner Mountain Bear, out of maiden sister to 7f Group 3 winner Ishvana; selected by McMonagle from stable's two debutantes.
runners.comment: 100,000euros yearling; half-sister to Italian 2yo winner Ancient Story; McMonagle rides

runners.name: Lady Patrona
runners.spotlight: Made 11,000gns as a yearling; shaped as though she would improve the experience when runner-up on debut at Naas (5f, good); this trip can help but others preferred.
runners.comment: Shaped as though she would improve for experience when runner-up on debut at Naas
runners.owner: Miss Kate Harrington & Mrs John Harrington & B Phelan

runners.name: Snowing
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never filly; 950,000gns Y; seventh foal; closely related to 7f/1m winner Maljoom (inc AW/Group 2: RPR 122), half-sister to HK 7f winner Maximize Heart (63); dam unraced half-sister to Listed-placed winners Roof Fiddle (5f 2yo) and Sylvestris (7f)
runners.comment: 950,000gns yearling purchase; closely related to Maljoom (RPR 122); Moore rides another
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Green Empress
runners.spotlight: 55,000euros yearling; second foal; half-sister to US 1m turf 2yo winner Amberglen; dam 1m/1m2f AW winner (inc 2yo; RPR 98), sister to winners Sea Tsarina (7f/1m), Sea Regal (1m2f) and Red Line Alexander (9.4f), out of 8.6f winner; McMonagle rides Akari.
runners.comment: 55,000euros yearling; half-sister to US 1m turf 2yo winner; Akari is stable number one

runners.name: Heaven's Glory
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly; sister to 7f 2yo/11.6f Listed winner Cameo (RPR 105), half-sister to 7f 2yo/1m4f Listed winner Portland (106); dam Group-placed 1m 2yo winner (103), half-sister to 6.5f Group 1 winner Garswood; Moore rides Sun Goddess.
runners.comment: Wootton Bassett filly; sister to Cameo (RPR 105); Moore rides Sun Goddess
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Pala Aurelia
runners.spotlight: 230,000gns breeze-up purchase; upset in the stalls at Naas when finishing last of five, but it wasn't a run totally devoid of promise; blinkers go on now and a fair chunk of improvement needed.
runners.comment: Last of five at Naas on debut when upset in stalls; a lot of improvement needed

runners.name: Great Barrier Reef
runners.spotlight: Mentioned positively by his trainer at a press event launching the Flat season, he didn't let him down when impressively winning over C&D last month (6f, soft, 4-9); ran greenly that day, so still room for improvement; breeding suggests better ground will cause no issues.
runners.comment: Impressive debut C&D winner; should handle better ground; big player

runners.name: Immortal Guard
runners.spotlight: Saw off the closing Otherworldly on C&D debut (good); entitled to improve on that, but stable's number one is Edward Thatch.
runners.comment: Saw off Otherwordly on C&D debut; entitled to improve but stable selected is Edward Thatch
runners.owner: Ramiro Restrepo & Jacob West & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Carry The Flag
runners.spotlight: Made a pleasing debut when runner-up here over 5f (soft); didn't win as easily as the market suggested at Naas last time (30-100), getting up late to beat an 80-1 chance by a head; the step up to 6f will suit, but Great Barrier Reef is the more attractive of the trainer's two.
runners.comment: Pleasing debut here but made heavy weather of winning at Naas last time; 6f will suit

runners.name: Lars Soldier
runners.spotlight: Massive improvement over his 5f debut here (heavy) in March when landing a maiden over the same distance by a nose last time (soft to heavy), beating Edward Thatch in third who ran too keenly and Confucius in second; trainer believes he will handle better ground but may struggle to confirm the form of his win.
runners.comment: Hugely improved over debut when beating Edward Thatch last time; unraced on better ground

runners.name: Alaskan Bear
runners.spotlight: Kodiac colt ran a fine race on debut to chase home the Coventry Stakes-bound Ballydoyle colt Confucius at Naas (6f, good); pitched into Group 3 company now and others are more appealing on form.
runners.comment: Ran a fine race on debut when runner-up; others more appealing on form

runners.name: Otherworldly
runners.spotlight: Finished 0.5l behind Immortal Guard over C&D on debut (good), denied a clear run on that occasion before making up late ground; should be close between the pair once again but others preferred for win purposes.
runners.comment: Closely matched with Immortal Guard from their C&D debut but others preferred

runners.name: Brazilian Diva
runners.spotlight: Cost 12,000 euros as a yearling; well beaten on debut at Dundalk (5f); this looks ambitious.
runners.comment: Well beaten on debut at Dundalk and this looks ambitious

races.race_name: BYD At Finlay Motor Group Spring Fillies Handicap

runners.name: White Sand Beach
runners.spotlight: Placed in all four maiden attempts; dropped in class after a Group 3 bid in which she failed to distinguish herself; has a superb pedigree and deserves respect in this grade.
runners.comment: Placed in all four maiden attempts, dropped in class after Group 3 bid, deserves respect
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Mayflower
runners.spotlight: Debut juvenile winner; dropped in class after two Group 3 runs; not beaten far last time in Athasi Stakes dominated by Irish 1,000 Guineas candidates Kensingston Lane and Black Caviar Gold; big burden, 3lb claimed.
runners.comment: Debut juvenile winner, dropped in class after two Group 3 runs, not beaten far last time

runners.name: Anushka
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Dundalk fillies' maiden last October; failed to make an impact when highly tried on reappearance in a Group 3 race won by True Love; not far off Green Carrera and Prevalence over 7f here in her first handicap.
runners.comment: AW winner, not far off Green Carrera and Prevalence over 7f here in her first handicap

runners.name: Punica Granatum
runners.spotlight: Showed winning potential in making the frame in 7f maidens here and at Gowran on her first two starts; faced a tough task in the Goffs Million; makes handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Showed winning potential on her first two starts, faced a tough task in the Goffs Million

runners.name: Expert Dancer
runners.spotlight: Consistent in maidens; third on latest in a 7f Limerick race won by the Aidan O'Brien-trained Signora who was second to good yardstick Signora in a Group 3 race last time; visored for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Consistent in maidens, third on latest in a race won by a filly who is now Group 3-placed

runners.name: Liberation Date
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f maiden at Dundalk last December; subsequent AW run looked too bad to be true; raises a doubt all the same.
runners.comment: Won a 7f maiden at Dundalk last December, subsequent AW run looked too bad to be true

runners.name: Prevalence
runners.spotlight: Dundalk winner on second start at two; third in a 7f handicap here on seasonal debut, a performance that can be upgraded on more than one count, since she was slowly away, raced quite keenly, and was not given a hard race in the end; only a short head behind Green Carrera and may turn the tables.
runners.comment: Has almost precisely the same strong chance as Green Carrera on running here last month

runners.name: Green Carrera
runners.spotlight: Thurles maiden winner last season over 1m; final run at two was a Listed race at Pontefract; ran on strongly for second over 7f at this track on handicap debut; closely matched with Prevalence, with the pair both likely to improve.
runners.comment: Ran on strongly over 7f at this track on handicap debut, closely matched with Prevalence

runners.name: Cherry Hill Girl
runners.spotlight: Placed in a nursery after a Listowel maiden win, out of the money in two runs this term; others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Placed in a nursery after a Listowel maiden win, out of the money in two runs this term

runners.name: Stars Will Shine
runners.spotlight: Down Royal maiden winner last September; found wanting in a Group 3 race on heavy ground on final start; Oisin Murphy booked for handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Down Royal maiden winner last September, found wanting in a Group 3 race on final start

runners.name: Glamazon
runners.spotlight: Retains her existing mark after a Roscommon maiden win gained on her tenth outing; 7lb out of the handicap, that disadvantage somewhat offset by the rider's useful claim; vulnerable on overall form.
runners.comment: Retains existing mark after Roscommon maiden win leaving her 7lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Goomah
runners.spotlight: Some encouragement from her juvenile runs at Naas and Dundalk; beaten favourite in what looked an ordinary Leopardstown maiden on her recent reappearance; may have needed the run.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite in what looked an ordinary Leopardstown maiden, may have needed the run

runners.name: Jancis
runners.spotlight: Smart form in winning her first two races at three; failed to add to her tally last season; posted a career-best to win a 1m1f Group 2 event at Newmarket three weeks ago; has the beating of Francophone on that running; strong contender if she can replicate that form on slower ground; seems ideally suited by a quick surface.
runners.comment: Smart form at three, failed to win last year, posted career-best for recent Newmarket win

runners.name: City Of Memphis
runners.spotlight: Bolted up in a Cork maiden last May; thrown straight into Group 1 company in the Irish 1,000 Guineas and coped admirably, beaten 4 1/2l in fifth behind Lake Victoria, despite racing keenly; won over 7f at Gowran last month after a lengthy absence; backed that up with an emphatic Group 3 win at Leopardstown; can continue on the upgrade.
runners.comment: Has matured well since last year's Irish 1,000 Guineas fifth, recent Group 3 winner

runners.name: Stateira
runners.spotlight: Four wins on AW, latest in a valuable Newcastle handicap; needs to prove herself on turf; her single attempt (at Goodwood a year ago) was too bad to be true; has beaten Irish-trained handicappers on her last two starts; faces opposition of a much higher standard now.
runners.comment: Four wins on AW, latest in a valuable Newcastle handicap, needs to prove herself on turf

runners.name: One Look
runners.spotlight: C&D Group 3 winner last year, but seems most effective at 1m2f, judged on her placed efforts in the Blandford Stakes and the Prix de l'Opéra; stablemate of City Of Memphis who has the advantage of race fitness.
runners.comment: C&D Group 3 winner last year, seems most effective at 1m2f, stablemate of City Of Memphis

runners.name: Princess Child
runners.spotlight: Improved throughout last season, landing a competitive handicap at Galway over 7f and a Group 3 event at Tipperary before another good effort when fourth in a 1m Group 2 at Longchamp; beaten by stablemate Celestial Orbit in a 7f Group 3 here last month; held by City Of Memphis on running a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Beaten by Celestial Orbit in 7f Group 3 here, held by City Of Memphis on recent running

runners.name: Carolina Jetstream
runners.spotlight: Useful AW performer, all four wins at Dundalk; inferior to stablemate California Dreamer.
runners.comment: Useful AW performer, all four wins at Dundalk, inferior to stablemate California Dreamer
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Francophone
runners.spotlight: Twice a Listed winner, notably when beating Hey Boo over this trip at Newmarket on her final start last season; appears well held by Jancis based on a Group 2 clash at the same venue three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Twice a Listed winner, appears well held by Jancis based on a Group 2 clash at Newmarket

runners.name: Quebec
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in Pattern races in Germany last season; failed to make an impression behind City Of Memphis on Irish debut.
runners.comment: Useful form in Germany, failed to make an impression behind City Of Memphis on Irish debut

runners.name: Sindria
runners.spotlight: Improving filly; two wins at Gowran this term; her trainer has two stronger candidates in Celestial Orbit and Princess Child.
runners.comment: Improving filly, two wins at Gowran this term, her trainer has two stronger candidates

runners.name: California Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner; stand-out turf display was her second to Lake Victoria in Irish 1,000 Guineas (City Of Memphis fifth); failed to go on from that run last season, and now 0-15 on turf; struggled in race won by Celestial Orbit here early last month; better effort before fading into fourth behind City Of Memphis on latest.
runners.comment: Last year's Irish 1,000 Guineas second, faded into fourth behind City Of Memphis on latest
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Hey Boo
runners.spotlight: Last season's Fred Darling runner-up; ended the campaign with a Newmarket second behind Francophone; lacks a recent outing and faces a stiff task in the circumstances.
runners.comment: Ended last season with a Newmarket second behind Francophone, lacks a recent outing

runners.name: Celestial Orbit
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Ollie Sangster at two, including a 7f Listed race at Sandown; began and ended last season with good runs in defeat (in the Nell Gwyn and a Listed contest at Saint-Cloud); sprang a surprise when beating stablemate Princess Child in a C&D Group 3 event on Irish debut; further progress is feasible.
runners.comment: Sprang a surprise when beating stablemate Princess Child on Irish debut, could go well
runners.owner: J P Magnier/Mrs P Shanahan/Mrs M V Magnier/K MacLennan

runners.name: Minnie Hauk
runners.spotlight: Last year's top-rated filly worldwide; won the Cheshire Oaks, the Classics at Epsom and the Curragh and the Yorkshire Oaks; at least matched her best effort when touched off by Daryz on soft in the Arc, well clear of the remainder; perhaps one race too many when underwhelming at the Breeders' Cup; picked up smoothly to win a Group 2 by 1.75l here on seasonal return (1m2f, good); this is a bigger task, and it's not unfair to say her ideal trip is further than this, but she's the most likely winner.
runners.comment: Last year's top-rated filly won here on seasonal return and is the most likely winner
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Saddadd
runners.spotlight: Rose through the ranks last season, winning back-to-back handicaps at Newbury (1m2f, good to firm) and Sandown (1m2f, soft); took the next step forward when landing Group 3 honours at Sandown (1m2f, good) on seasonal return (a race commonly won by high-class performers); enters the big league now but further improvement is by no means ruled out for a four-year-old having just his eighth start.
runners.comment: Dual-h'cap winner last term landed Gr.3 honours on seasonal return; in the big league now

runners.name: Bay City Roller
runners.spotlight: Showed some high-class form last year, including when beaten less than a length by Daryz in the Prix Egene Adam; hit the big time when making all to win the Group 1 Grosser Allianz Preis von Bayern by 7l at Munich (1m4f, soft) in November; huge run at Chester when conceding weight all around, just going down by a neck to last year's dual Derby winner Lambourn; ideally suited by softer ground and a bit to find on ratings but he's clearly in top form.
runners.comment: High-class form last year and won a German Gr.1 in Nov'; top effort at Chester latest

runners.name: Almaqam
runners.spotlight: Closest to Minnie Hauk on ratings, although that rival has the benefit of race-fitness and a fillies allowance; made all to beat subsequent three-time Group 1 winner Ombudsman at Sandown (1m2f, good) last season but had the benefit of a run; good placed efforts in Group 2s at York (10.5f, good) and Longchamp (1m4f, good to soft); signed off last term with a cracking run to take third in a Group 1 at Ascot on Champions Day (1m2f, good); there is likely more to come to this term from the British raider.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner and ran a cracker on final start last term in Gr.1 company; lacks a run

runners.name: Edward Hamilton
runners.spotlight: Finished last in two starts following his Dundalk maiden win; much improved effort as a pacemaker to Minnie Hauk at the Curragh last time, when holding onto the runner-up spot; supporting role here once again.
runners.comment: Improved to take second when Minnie Hauk's pacemaker here last time; supporting role again
runners.owner: P M Brant & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Green Impact
runners.spotlight: Beat the top-class Delacroix in a Leopardstown Group 2 as a 2yo (1m, good); sixth in the 2,000 Guineas before making all in a Leopardstown Listed race in June (1m1f, good); respectable efforts in the Irish Derby here (1m4f, good) and in a York Group 2 (10.5f, good); shaped as though he would improve over his return in a Group 3 here last time when fading into fourth (1m2f, soft); a good bit to find with the top ones here all the same.
runners.comment: Shaped as though he would improve over comeback here when fourth; a good bit to find still

runners.name: Tiberius Thunder
runners.spotlight: Showed improved effort here last time when beaten 6.5l behind Minnie Hauk (1m2f, good); that was a step in the right direction but it's still hard to see him playing any major role.
runners.comment: His 6.5l defeat behind Minnie Hauk last time was better, but hard to see him featuring
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Royal Rhyme
runners.spotlight: Did well early in his career, including when beating three rivals in the 2024 Brigadier Gerard at Sandown (1m2f, soft); 0-9 since, albeit twice placed at the highest level; pulled too hard when beaten 6.25l into sixth of 12 on return in a Group 3 here last month; never featured at Chester in a Group 2 last time when the ground was likely too quick; similarly good ground likely here and would need a huge performance.
runners.comment: Last win came in 2024 but placed twice at highest level since; better ground against him
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: True Love
runners.spotlight: Queen Mary winner; showed top-class form over 6f later in the season; had American Queen behind in fourth when winning the Cheveley Park; beating Magny Cours and Pivotal Attack readily over 7f on reappearance; travelled strongly and stayed on powerfully to win the 1,000 Guineas; had the advantage of race fitness over stablemate Precise who should get closer now.
runners.comment: Cheveley Park winner, leap-frogged Precise in winning the 1,000 Guineas, can uphold form
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Precise
runners.spotlight: Dual Group 1 winner at two, landing the Moyglare and Fillies' Mile, bringing up a four-timer; drew clear inside the final furlong and comfortably beat Venetian Lace by just over 3l in the Newmarket race; failed to confirm form with Venetian Lace (third) in the 1,000 Guineas, and has been leap-frogged by True Love in the Ballydoyle reassessment.
runners.comment: Dual Group 1 winner at two, now relegated to stable second-string after Newmarket seventh

runners.name: Abashiri
runners.spotlight: By Frankel out of a Prix Saint Alary winner; quickened well for a 4l debut success in Kempton maiden (1m, AW) last November; gave a fine display for one so inexperienced when fifth (third of eight in group) in the 1,000 Guineas; open to improvement; place chance.
runners.comment: Fine effort for one so inexperienced when fifth in the 1,000 Guineas, place chance

runners.name: Kensington Lane
runners.spotlight: Flopped in a Group 3 race won by Black Caviar Gold on final start of a six-race juvenile campaign; finished in front of that below-par rival on reappearance; again had the better of the exchange when beating her in the Athasi Stakes over C&D; up against it here.
runners.comment: Beat the shorter-priced Black Caviar Gold in the Athasi Stakes over C&D, big task here

runners.name: America Queen
runners.spotlight: Did well over 6f last season, scoring by a wide margin at Haydock before second in the Lowther and fourth to True Love in the Cheveley Park; made a pleasing return when beating all bar outsider Azleet in the Nell Gwyn; that was her first attempt at 7f and advertised her chance of staying another furlong.
runners.comment: Second in the Lowther, fourth to True Love in the Cheveley Park, pleasing 7f run on return

runners.name: Beautify
runners.spotlight: 6f Group 2 winner here last summer and close second to Precise in the Moyglare; well beaten in the Cheveley Park and held by American Queen on Nell Gwyn running.
runners.comment: Second to Precise in the Moyglare, well held by American Queen on Nell Gwyn running
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Black Caviar Gold
runners.spotlight: Showed huge improvement to win a 7f Group 3 at this venue last September (soft), involving a reversal of form with Pivotal Attack; loss of a shoe probably damaged her chance on return in Leopardstown Group 3 won by True Love; better judged on recent second to Kensington Lane over C&D; a lot more needed now.
runners.comment: Lost a shoe in a Group 3 won by True Love, solid form in Pattern races at this venue

runners.name: Sukanya
runners.spotlight: Fourth behind Precise at Goodwood last summer; ran her best race in Ireland when second to the top-class Diamond Necklace over 7f at Leopardstown in September; a game winner of the Fred Darling last month, beating favourite Touleen, the subsequent 1,000 Guineas sixth; has a good chance of seeing out this trip but needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: A game winner of the Fred Darling last month, has a good chance of seeing out this trip

runners.name: Sinmara
runners.spotlight: Gowran maiden winner; weakened into fourth in the race won in which Kensington Lane beat Black Caviar Gold; official rating of 94 leaves her a long way short of the standard required.
runners.comment: Official rating of 94 leaves her a long way short of the standard required

runners.name: Pivotal Attack
runners.spotlight: Galway maiden winner on yielding; not disgraced in the Moyglare when racing keenly; ended the season with a positive effort behind Black Caviar Gold in a 7f Group 3 at this venue; started the season brightly with third behind True Love and Magny Cours at Leopardstown
runners.comment: Started the season brightly with third behind True Love and Magny Cours at Leopardstown

runners.name: Magny Cours
runners.spotlight: Talented maiden (stripped of a Listowel win after a positive test); best form when second to smart colt Alparslan in a sales race at this venue, and when runner-up in a 7f Group event at Chantilly; deserves a shot at this based on her second to True Love at Leopardstown six weeks ago.
runners.comment: Talented maiden, deserves a shot at this based on her second to True Love at Leopardstown

runners.name: Hope Queen
runners.spotlight: Backed up debut win at Beverley when scoring a narrow success in a 7f Listed race at Sandown last July; form boosted this season by the second Jennifer Jane, a wide-margin winner of Newmarket's Pretty Polly; pedigree implies she can get the trip.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in two runs at two, beat a subsequent Group 3 winner in a Listed race at Sandown

runners.name: Navagio
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner but losing run stretches back to July 2023; recent Roscommon sixth was mildly encouraging; plenty more needed now.
runners.comment: Almost three years since his last win, recent Roscommon sixth was mildly encouraging

runners.name: Summer Snow
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicaps at 7f/1m last season; probably a bit high in the ratings now; big price when towards the rear at Leopardstown on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Progressive in handicaps at 7f/1m last season, probably a bit high in the ratings now

runners.name: Thaloria
runners.spotlight: Stepped up markedly on maiden runs when scoring on handicap debut at Dundalk in January over 7f; needs to step up on the form of her Leopardstown run last month; stable's Summer Island is probably more progressive,
runners.comment: Dundalk winner, needs to step up on the form of her Leopardstown run last month

runners.name: Albion Power
runners.spotlight: Both wins gained at Dundalk, where he has done most of his racing; turf experience limited to two runs last year, and neither was encouraging.
runners.comment: Both wins gained at Dundalk where he has done most of his racing, limited turf experience

runners.name: Grecian Power
runners.spotlight: Outran 33-1 odds on belated debut in a 7f maiden here last July, missing the break and staying on nicely for third in a 21-runner contest; has failed to build on that in two subsequent starts; potentially interesting with a tongue-tie fitted for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Much the best of her three maiden runs was over 7f here last July on debut, interesting
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs S Power

runners.name: Zaraahmando
runners.spotlight: Three wins last season at 7f-1m on good and soft ground, going up a total of 21lb; proved he remained competitive when fourth on return at Naas (7f, soft); has to bounce back from a below-par effort at Roscommon 13 days ago.
runners.comment: Three wins last year, started new season with good Naas run, below-par effort at Roscommon

runners.name: Merlin The Wizard
runners.spotlight: Three wins in Britain in 2023 at up to 1m; regressive in the last two seasons; produced best run for some time at Dundalk in March when good second over 1m; not beaten far in outings at Leopardstown and Ballinrobe since then.
runners.comment: Three wins in Britain in 2023, has shown something like his best form in recent months

runners.name: Spanish Temptress
runners.spotlight: Went up an aggregate 21lb for three handicap wins last season; continuing to improve, judged on Limerick second last month, beaten only a nose; has picked up another 2lb for that but still worth considering.
runners.comment: Progressive three-time winner last year, continuing to improve judged on Limerick second

runners.name: Neo Smart
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f handicap here last year, also won at Dundalk in February; not the most consistent and finished behind Highbury See See; closely matched with Navagio.
runners.comment: Won a 7f handicap here last year, also won at Dundalk in February, not the most consistent

runners.name: Pretty Omagh Girl
runners.spotlight: Some promise in three maiden runs last year; beaten favourite at Dundalk on final start; lack of a recent outing could be a negative.
runners.comment: Some promise in three maiden runs last year, beaten favourite at Dundalk on final start

runners.name: Liberate
runners.spotlight: Won 1m2f Goodwood novice last year but made no impact afterwards in handicaps; weak form over longer distances since joining this stable.
runners.comment: Weak form over longer distances since joining this stable, won over 1m2f in Britain

runners.name: Banner Beauty
runners.spotlight: Won Leopardstown maiden (1m, good) on seasonal/yard debut last May; failed to make a significant impact in two 2025 handicap runs.
runners.comment: Leopardstown maiden winner, failed to make a significant impact in two 2025 handicap runs
runners.owner: O B P Carroll & Anthony Vaughan

runners.name: Summer Island
runners.spotlight: AW winner in February on stable debut; stepped up from Bellewstown third when winning readily over 7f here early in the month; should be in the mix again despite 9lb rise, but not guaranteed to confirm with fifth-placed Java Wood.
runners.comment: Plenty in hand when notching a first turf win over 7f here, 9lb higher, gets this trip
runners.owner: Nigel O'Hare & Martin W White & Mark Devlin & C G Bryson

runners.name: Hexagonal
runners.spotlight: Maiden with a little enough mileage on the clock; gave an improved display when second at Ballinrobe last time; others have stronger overall claims.
runners.comment: Gave an improved display when second at Ballinrobe last time, others have stronger claims

runners.name: Va Va Vroom
runners.spotlight: Out of form for Jessica Harrington last year; no sign of a revival in two runs this term; this looks too demanding.
runners.comment: Out of form for Jessica Harrington last year, no sign of a revival in two runs this term

runners.name: No More Porter
runners.spotlight: Reasonably consistent on AW late last year; has a modest overall strike rate (3-56), but worth bearing in mind that all his wins have come at this track.
runners.comment: Consistent on AW late last year, modest overall strike rate (3-56), all wins at this track

runners.name: Highbury See See
runners.spotlight: Both wins last year came on soft and heavy ground; fair fourth on good at Roscommon last time, and this ground should be fine for him.
runners.comment: Last season's C&D win came on very soft ground, fair fourth on good at Roscommon last time

runners.name: Java Wood
runners.spotlight: Modest form in turf maidens before 40-1 win on AW/handicap debut last August; made a pleasing return when staying on for fifth of 15 over 7f at this venue; every chance now after another solid display when fourth at Leopardstown.
runners.comment: Long-priced Dundalk winner last year, has run with credit in two turf handicaps this term

runners.name: Blues Emperor
runners.spotlight: Five wins for Johnny Murtagh; signs of a return to form when fourth of 17 here on latest; now 10lb below his last winning mark; one for the short list.
runners.comment: Five wins for Johnny Murtagh, signs of a return to form when fourth of 17 here on latest

runners.name: Kingstonian
runners.spotlight: Well-backed to make it a successful stable debut at Chester (2m3f, good) when the line came too soon; previously won a Tramore maiden (1m4f, good to yielding) for Jessica Harrington; up 3lb for his Chester exertions but the choice of McMonagle and could improve over that seasonal return.
runners.comment: Just failed at Chester; up 3lb for that but McMonagle rides and could improve

runners.name: Trubshaw
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season, both of them under today's rider and the second of which arrived over C&D (yielding); creditable enough seasonal return off 9lb higher at Navan when a 5.5l eighth of 20, shaping as though he would improve for the run; in the mix.
runners.comment: Dual winner last term inc' over C&D and made a creditable seasonal return; in the mix

runners.name: Sea Coral
runners.spotlight: Bellewstown maiden winner in July (1m4f, good); her seasonal return at Cork was not encouraging, nor was her handicap debut at Dundalk in October; hood on now; stable has better candidates.
runners.comment: Her seasonal return was not encouraging; hood goes on

runners.name: Kilmeaden
runners.spotlight: Winner of a Galway festival handicap off 82 in July (1m5f, yielding), beating a subsequent Group 3 winner; added a hurdles win in a Punchestown maiden in January (2m, soft to heavy); never on terms on return to the Flat at Cork last time when seventh.
runners.comment: Galway festival h'cap winner off 82, beating subsequent Gr.3 winner; never on terms latest

runners.name: Steps In The Sand
runners.spotlight: Notched a three-timer last summer with wins at Bellewstown (1m7f, good), Ballinrobe (1m5f, good to yielding) and Galway (1m6f, yielding); made a moderate return at Ballinrobe last time and regular rider Patrick McGettigan is not aboard.
runners.comment: Notched a three-timer last summer, but his time may come later in the season

runners.name: Marazion
runners.spotlight: Makes her handicap debut off the back of gaining her first maiden win on her eighth career start at Roscommon last time (1m4f, good); her opening mark looks fair on what she has achieved; in the mix.
runners.comment: Makes h'cap debut off back of m'den win at eighth attempt; opening mark looks fair

runners.name: Genoah
runners.spotlight: Four wins on the AW, has only raced five times on turf; plenty to prove on this surface.
runners.comment: Four-time AW winner has plenty to prove on this surface

runners.name: Robbies Rock
runners.spotlight: Won this last year off 78 to notch his second Flat win in first-time cheekpieces; no surprise to see him leave his exceedingly moderate Gowran return behind back on good ground with the cheekpieces back on.
runners.comment: Last year's winner off 78; no surprise to see him leave moderate return well behind

runners.name: Delta
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in a 1m4f Fairyhouse maiden in June having been third in a quality Navan Listed race the time before; heavy ground a possible excuse when disappointing off top weight at Listowel in September; gelded since and he went close on his seasonal return last year.
runners.comment: Took third in a good Listed race before winning his m'den; gelded since last seen

runners.name: Maeve Brennan
runners.spotlight: Decent efforts in three maidens here last term and ran a decent race here on handicap debut in March when fifth of 14 (1m4f, soft to heavy); improved upon encountering good to yielding ground at Navan last time when second of 23 behind an easy winner; tries this new trip and not discounted.
runners.comment: Ran her best race so far at Navan on better ground last time; not discounted
runners.owner: Eleanora Kennedy & Mrs J S Bolger

runners.name: Ragmans Corner
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-9 on Flat with the win coming at Navan last June (1m6f, good) off 73; good hurdler and added a fourth win in that sphere at Killarney last time; not discounted.
runners.comment: 1-9 on Flat and added fourth hurdles win at Killarney last time; not discoutned

runners.name: Come On The Lads
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner returns to this sphere for the first time since a midfield finish at Laytown in September (7f); must bounce back from a moderate run over fences at Wexford last time.
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner won a Limerick chase in March; poor effort over fences last time

runners.name: Kool One
runners.spotlight: Added a third Flat win at Navan last time (1m5f, good) in a big field by a neck; had Trubshaw 5.5l behind that day; up 5lb but should remain competitive; has never raced at this track.
runners.comment: Up 5lb for his Navan win, but should remain competitive

runners.name: Chutzpal
runners.spotlight: Capable under both codes, with dual wins in each; won a Killarney handicap in August and was a distant runner-up at Newmarket in September (8l behind Reverend Hubert); finished seventh in this race last year, and may need this comeback run once again.
runners.comment: Capable under both codes; seventh in this last year and may need the run once again

runners.name: Slurricane
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler who has won twice on the Flat, including off 4lb lower at Galway in October (1m4f, heavy); ran respectably over this trip at Gowran last month (soft); may prefer softer ground.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler has won twice on the Flat and ran okay last time; may prefer softer

runners.name: Carthusian
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 4yo was 0-5 in maidens last year but showed a fair level of ability each time; shaped as though he would improve over seasonal return/handicap debut at Ballinrobe (1m5f, good to yielding) when fading into seventh.
runners.comment: Shaped as though she would improve for seasonal return/h'cap debut last time

runners.name: Storm Heart
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over hurdles has a 1-5 record on the level in France, winning over 1m4f on good ground; a bit below his best at Punchestown last time when seventh of 17 in a Listed handicap hurdle; interesting contender but others preferred.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdles winner is 1-5 on Flat; interesting contender, but others preferred

runners.name: Alba Chiara
runners.spotlight: All her best form has been at Down Royal, a win and two seconds; should appreciate better ground, but lacks a run.
runners.comment: Best form at Down Royal, a win and two seconds; will appreciate ground but lacks a run
runners.owner: Mrs Tracey O'Hare & D W Sloan & William Jones

runners.name: Lark In The Mornin
runners.spotlight: Good form on the Flat when last seen, taking third at Listowel before winning at 4-11 in a Bellewstown conditions race; more impressive was his head victory back at Listowel in September (1m6f, good to yielding); however, top weight and stable have better candidates.
runners.comment: In good form on Flat when last seen but top weight and stable have better candidates

runners.name: Westminster Moon
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner in Poland at up to 1m4f and placed in German Group 1 and Italian Group 2 in 2024; not fired since joining current yard, but his run at Cork last time when running on for seventh (1m3f, good to yielding) was eye-catching; check market.
runners.comment: Good form abroad but not fired since joining this yard; run last time was eye-catching

runners.name: Pavilion End
runners.spotlight: Has only had one Flat run on turf; fair performer at up to 2m on AW at Dundalk, maiden winner (1m4f) and placed twice, including when third off this mark in October (1m4f); should be suited by ground conditions.
runners.comment: Fair performer on AW but only one turf Flat run; ground should suit

runners.name: High Honour
runners.spotlight: Sole win on Flat came in 2023, although has mostly been competing over hurdles in recent times; improved markedly over poor Navan return when fourth at Roscommon last time (1m4f, good), staying on after being slowly away; down to a career low mark.
runners.comment: Positive fourth at Roscommon latest; on career low mark and might not be badly treated

runners.name: Spanish John
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose sort won twice on the level last season at Tipperary and Bellewstown over an extended 1m4f on softish ground; tailed off over hurdles at Fairyhouse when last seen and 3lb wrong.
runners.comment: Won twice on the level last season but tailed off over hurdles latest and 3lb wrong

runners.name: Highwind
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose sort's sole Flat win came on the AW; best run on turf was a 2l fourth off 1lb lower on latest in the Ulster Cesarewitch at Down Royal (2m3f, good); may need this after 261 days away; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Sole Flat win came on AW and may need this after 261 days away

runners.name: Reverend Hubert
runners.spotlight: Drew 8l clear in Cesarewitch trial at Newmarket in September (2m2f, good); couldn't follow up in the real thing when sixth of 19; up 10lb compared to his last win and he has never won after an absence before.
runners.comment: Up 10lb compared to his easy Newmarket win; has never won after an absence before

races.race_name: Wrights Rolling In The Dough Derby Mares' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: First Candidate
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who built on her early hurdling promise with a comfortable defeat of a subsequent winner at Haydock (1m7f, good; 1-4); she was entitled to win that and she's entitled to win this, even with a penalty.
runners.comment: Entitled to win comfortably at Hadock and entitled to win this, even with a penalty

runners.name: Blue Skies Ahead
runners.spotlight: Won her bumper on good ground at Hexham and probably improved again to be second in a maiden hurdle there 22 days ago; this longer trip won't be an issue and she may be the one to give First Candidate something to think about.
runners.comment: Last two runs at Hexham, winning a bumper and then an improved second over hurdles
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

runners.name: Pretty Flamingo
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to smart bumper and 2m4f/2m7f hurdle winner Augusta Kate; dam a bumper and high-class 2m hurdle winner; this is a belated debut but she has a good pedigree and has to be of interest.
runners.comment: This is a belated debut but she has a good pedigree and has to be of interest

runners.name: Port And Brandy
runners.spotlight: 1-5 in Irish points, the win coming on yielding to soft ground; beaten 4l (heavy) and 9l (soft) in her two bumpers; probably up against it on this hurdle debut and the ground could be an issue.
runners.comment: 1-5 in Irish points; beaten 4l (heavy) and 9l (soft) in her two bumpers

runners.name: Britway Belle
runners.spotlight: Bought for £120,000 after winning an Irish mares' maiden point; progressive in bumpers, beaten 3.5l in the latest at Ludlow (good); should benefit from this longer distance now sent over hurdles.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; progressive in bumpers, beaten 3.5l in the latest at Ludlow

runners.name: Pigeon Forge
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner; well behind on two visits to Hereford (bumper/maiden) and questionable what she achieved in chasing home an easy winner at Plumpton (2m4f, good); has to raise her game you would think.
runners.comment: Questionable what she achieved in chasing home an easy winner at Plumpton

runners.name: Davita Blaze
runners.spotlight: Pretty much tailed off in a soft-ground bumper and two maidens on good; has no form claims.
runners.comment: Pretty much tailed off in a soft-ground bumper and two maidens on good; has no form claims

runners.name: Callisto Queen
runners.spotlight: Never threatened the principals in two bumpers on good to soft but she's well bred and entitled to do better over hurdles.
runners.comment: Never threatened the principals in two bumpers but should make a better hurdler

races.race_name: Wrights Great British Bake Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Galway Reel
runners.spotlight: Won a chase over 3m in September and nearly won another two runs back; produced a solid effort in defeat on his return to hurdling here last week and Sean Bowen rides again.
runners.comment: Produced a solid effort in defeat on his return to hurdling here last week

runners.name: Corsican Caper
runners.spotlight: Lots of Flat racing and lowly rated these days; hadn't shown much over hurdles but kept finding to finish a 5.5l third over 2m4f here 22 days ago on his handicap debut, and this longer trip looks the obvious move.
runners.comment: High Flat mileage; third here 22 days ago and worth a go over this longer trip

runners.name: Izzy's Grey
runners.spotlight: No wins in 15 but has performed well enough on the odd occasion to merit some respect in a race of this nature; trip/ground fine; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Performed well enough on the odd occasion to merit some respect in a race of this nature

runners.name: Cinnodin
runners.spotlight: Multiple Flat winner who was contesting only his second handicap over hurdles when winning with the front two clear over C&D (good) three weeks ago; a 6lb rise isn't the end of the world by any means.
runners.comment: Recent C&D winner and a 6lb rise isn't the end of the world by any means

runners.name: Dublin City
runners.spotlight: 0-3 in points and failed to raise his modest profile when 50-1 for handicap debut at Bangor (2m7f, good); no appeal for now.
runners.comment: Failed to raise his modest profile when 50-1 for handicap debut at Bangor (2m7f)
runners.owner: Mrs J A Benson & Miss S N Benson

races.race_name: Wrights Centenary Gold Cup Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Percy Shelley
runners.spotlight: Three Flat wins and he's 2-5 over hurdles; competitive in both disciplines since returning from a break and this scenario is ideal for him.
runners.comment: Consistent performer under both codes; trip-and-ground combination is ideal

runners.name: Bowmore
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly at about 2m but over this far he ran well for a 125-1 chance to be beaten about 10l in a big-field handicap at the Grand National meeting (under Daire McConville; finished ahead of Sir Galahad); this is not quite so demanding.
runners.comment: Beaten 10l in a big field at the Grand National meeting and this isn't quite so demanding

runners.name: Liverpool Knight
runners.spotlight: Plenty of good runs to his name since his last win in September 2023 and he raced freely after a break when behind Miss Maverick and Sir Galahad here 22 days ago; better can be expected this time.
runners.comment: Behind two of these here recently but he did race very freely after a break

runners.name: Service Minimum
runners.spotlight: Has improved for good ground in winning her last two races (2m3f/2m5f), both with plenty in hand; raised 10lb for last time but she's only raced 13 times and is clearly on the upgrade.
runners.comment: Raised 10lb for last time but she's only raced 13 times and is clearly improving

runners.name: Sir Galahad
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite over C&D this month but Miss Maverick did get first run on him and he has a 5lb pull on the winner now; remains relatively unexposed over this far.
runners.comment: Miss Maverick got first run on him here recently and he has a 5lb pull on the winner now

runners.name: Flying Fortune
runners.spotlight: Won four on the trot at about 2m4f in 2024, culminating in a Grade 2 novice; hasn't recaptured that form since returning from an absence and she's in another competitive race here.
runners.comment: Smart in 2024 but hasn't recaptured that form since returning from an absence

runners.name: Champagne Kid
runners.spotlight: Irish challenger who has been contesting valuable handicaps back home, bagging one in new cheekpieces at Leopardstown during the festive period (2m4f, good to yielding); back at this trip (had tried 3m), it wasn't a bad run at Punchestown this month when a tongue-tie was added; hard to discount.
runners.comment: Irish challenger who has been contesting valuable handicaps back home

runners.name: Miss Maverick
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on this card last year and made it 5-9 here when making all to defeat Sir Galahad by 3l three weeks ago; 6lb rise sees her on a career-high mark but lots to like otherwise.
runners.comment: On a career-high mark but recent win here made it 5-9 at the track

runners.name: Rialannah
runners.spotlight: 2-3 for this yard after 2m7f/2m4f good-ground wins around Fakenham; dictated modest fractions the last time and this will be tougher off 4lb higher, but she doesn't have to lead and has to be respected.
runners.comment: 2-3 for this yard after 2m7f/2m4f good-ground wins around Fakenham

runners.name: Break Point
runners.spotlight: Has only won a maiden hurdle, when trained by Olly Murphy; two modest runs in low-grade AW races since leaving that yard.
runners.comment: Two modest runs in low-grade AW races since leaving Olly Murphy

runners.name: No Ordinary Joe
runners.spotlight: Off a reduced mark he won over 2m4f on good ground at Sandown and some interference didn't help matters when well beaten off this 5lb higher mark in the Swinton; still well treated on old form.
runners.comment: Won at Sandown and had excuses at Haydock; still well treated on old form

races.race_name: Clarke Chase (A Handicap Chase) (For The Sir Stanley And Lady Clarke Challenge Trophy) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Riskintheground
runners.spotlight: Good ground is the key to him; his form had nosedived but he was running much better when hampered late on at the Cheltenham Festival and then came good at Haydock (2m4f, good), prevailing only narrowly but under a confident ride in a strong race (he also won it the season before when staged at Cheltenham); a 4lb rise seems very generous.
runners.comment: It was never in much doubt at Haydock despite the neck margin; only up 4lb

runners.name: American Mike
runners.spotlight: Classy customer in these colours for Gordon Elliott; only his fourth run for current yard when forcing a dead-heat at Perth (3m, good; 3lb lower) and he's versatile trip-wise; likely contender in new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: His fourth run for current yard when forcing a dead-heat at Perth (3m, good; 3lb lower)

runners.name: Glengouly
runners.spotlight: Made all in major handicap at Cheltenham (2m4f) in December but hasn't completed any of his four subsequent races, the latest after wind surgery (and in a new visor); cheekpieces return and an easy lead would help matters; tailed off in this last year.
runners.comment: Tailed off in this last year and on another recovery mission now; too risky

runners.name: A Dublin Job
runners.spotlight: Handicap mark took a hit for his drawn-out hat-trick (third win came after 334 days off) and he's now back from another absence after pulling up on final appearance; very much up against it from 8lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Capable when fresh but he's very much up against it from 8lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Vintage Fizz
runners.spotlight: Ran quite well to be fourth in this last year when 7lb higher; however, he hasn't won since 2023 and the questions are mounting up.
runners.comment: He hasn't won since 2023 and the questions are mounting up
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing & T S Ingham

runners.name: Gunsight Ridge
runners.spotlight: Smart at his best for Olly Murphy but last season never got off the ground and he's moved yards.
runners.comment: Smart at his best for Olly Murphy but last season never got off the ground; moved yards

races.race_name: Wrights Sausage Roll Sprint Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Vocito
runners.spotlight: Exposed four-time winner over hurdles but now 2lb below the mark he defied here last summer; could be sharper for his recent run after a break and Sean Bowen replaces a 10lb claimer.
runners.comment: Could be sharper for his recent run after a break and Sean Bowen replaces a 10lb claimer

runners.name: A Different Kind
runners.spotlight: Without a win since scoring over fences in September 2023 and he was 2-7 favourite on that occasion; however, hasn't had a lot of racing since and produced three fair runs during winter off his lower hurdle mark; last run was a backward step but he's no forlorn hope with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Last time was disappointing but had produced three fair runs off his lower hurdle mark

runners.name: Fandabidozi
runners.spotlight: Two wins and a number of places to his name over hurdles; only 2l away on the AW 16 days ago when returning from a year off; handicapped to go well.
runners.comment: Good recent run on the AW after a year off; on a useful mark back over hurdles
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Gwyn Evans

runners.name: Turn And Finish
runners.spotlight: Became disappointing over hurdles for Harry Derham and well beaten on the AW and over hurdles for current yard; hood tried.
runners.comment: Well beaten on the AW and over hurdles for current yard; hood tried

runners.name: Politacus
runners.spotlight: Returns to hurdling after going 0-5 over fences, producing only one effort of real note; nothing wrong with her mark if getting back in this discipline floats her boat.
runners.comment: Nothing wrong with her mark if getting back in this discipline floats her boat

runners.name: Telhimlisten
runners.spotlight: Long breaks are a feature in his profile and he was returning from another when prevailing narrowly at Stratford two months ago (2m2f, good); if as good again off 4lb higher he'll be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Stratford winner two months ago; if as good again off 4lb higher he'll be thereabouts

runners.name: Call Me Tara
runners.spotlight: Last of three wins over hurdles was over 2m4f here (heavy; has also won on good) in July 2023 when she was left clear; confined to only two runs in the meantime and now returns from mammoth absence; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Course winner and 3-7 over hurdles, but she's returning from a mammoth absence

races.race_name: Wrights Always Tasty Classic Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Realisation
runners.spotlight: Won by 5l at Sedgefield (3m2f, good) but after a bold ride she was comfortably reeled in on her return visit; probably able off this mark if this race pans out more effectively.
runners.comment: Probably able off this mark if this race pans out more effectively than her last one

runners.name: Mahler Moon
runners.spotlight: Consistent gelding on his favoured good ground, winning twice this year in small fields; beaten 9l into second in another last time but there's a big run in him.
runners.comment: Consistent gelding on his favoured good ground, winning twice this year in small fields

runners.name: Scottish King
runners.spotlight: Completed his hat-trick over fences at Hexham (3m, soft), albeit in a race that fell apart at the seams; he's been plying his trade in Class 5s and now has to cope with a 9lb rise, but wouldn't put it past him.
runners.comment: On a four-timer; latest race did fall apart and that was a Class 5 off 9lb lower

runners.name: Kayce Dutton
runners.spotlight: Both wins over fences have been at Market Rasen (2m5f/3m); struggles with his consistency but if on a going day in first-time blinkers then he's not handicapped out of it.
runners.comment: If on a going day in first-time blinkers then he's not handicapped out of it

runners.name: Ballydisco
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who is 0-11 over fences but he has the form to feature off this mark if ready to rock on his stable debut after a long absence; the market should be informative.
runners.comment: Long absence to overcome but he has the form to feature off this mark

runners.name: Mr Griffiths
runners.spotlight: Won two of his four handicaps over hurdles and wasn't beaten far when fifth two months ago (over 3m on good); may prefer softer ground but brings potential to his chase career.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner; may prefer softer ground but brings potential to chase career

runners.name: Grand Clermont
runners.spotlight: Ex-French where all his winning was on heavy ground; weak in the betting ahead of British debut here (2m5f, good) and ended up tailed off.
runners.comment: Ex-French where all his winning was on heavy ground; tailed off on British debut

races.race_name: Wrights 100 Year Dash Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cruikshank
runners.spotlight: 52,000euros 3yo; fourth foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Puffin Billy (smart bumper and 2m-3m hurdle/chase), Zuzka (useful bumper and 2m2f-2m5f hurdle) and Tycoon Prince (useful bumper and 2m/2m1f hurdle/chase); nicely bred and in a top yard; market useful.
runners.comment: 52,000euros 3yo; nicely bred and in a top yard; market useful

runners.name: Sano City
runners.spotlight: From a Flat yard but half-brother to a winning hurdler; beaten by one with experience at Warwick (stablemate of Lucky Diamond; good) and that form is right up there in this company; every chance.
runners.comment: Beaten by one with experience at Warwick (good) and that form is right up there

runners.name: Ask Ernie
runners.spotlight: Closely related to 2m/2m4f hurdle winner Call Me Tara; dam point winner; stable has had occasional success in bumpers in recent seasons from not many runners.
runners.comment: Closely related to 2m/2m4f hurdle winner Call Me Tara; dam point winner

runners.name: Blinded By Grace
runners.spotlight: Close up on good ground at Worcester in September but that form hasn't worked out too well, while he'll want further in time; however, it's unlikely that this is a strong race.
runners.comment: Third at Worcester in September but that form hasn't worked out too well

runners.name: Show Your Hand
runners.spotlight: 66-1 and tailed off in a valuable Newbury bumper on debut; went close in a first-time tongue-tie at Exeter (good) but that did look a ropey race and the pace was slow.
runners.comment: Second at Exeter but that did look a ropey race and the pace was slow

runners.name: Lucky Diamond
runners.spotlight: Improved upon September's Worcester debut (2m, good to soft) when third there (2m, good) the following month; was due to contest a Cheltenham Listed race in November only be withdrawn on account of soft ground.
runners.comment: Sufficient promise in his second bumper run to believe he'll have a role to play

runners.name: Nobotheratall
runners.spotlight: Won a Carlisle bumper in October last year (2m1f, good) but hasn't done as well over hurdles (pulled hard when tailed off last time); looks one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Carlisle bumper winner last October; ordinary form over hurdles; looks one for handicaps

runners.name: Chemistry
runners.spotlight: Useful handicapper on the Flat; made a winning hurdles debut at Sedgefield last month (2m1f, good), beating an odds-on shot who won next time, the pair well clear; penalised; the one to beat.
runners.comment: Useful Flat handicapper who won at Sedgefield on hurdles debut; the one to beat
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Luna Grace
runners.spotlight: Third in three bumpers and also on hurdles debut at Musselburgh in March (2m1f, good to soft; second run after wind surgery); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Third on all starts so far, including on hurdles debut in March; each-way chance

runners.name: Terino
runners.spotlight: Doctor Dino 5yo; showed only minor promise when fourth in a point bumper in March; tongue-tie on; best watched on rules/stable debut.
runners.comment: Fourth in a point bumper on debut; tongue-tie on; best watched on stable/hurdle debut

runners.name: Osco Flyer
runners.spotlight: Irish raider; shaped with a degree of promise when 28l fifth of 14 in a maiden hurdle at Killarney this month (2m1f, good to yielding; 16-1); should improve; one to consider.
runners.comment: Irish raider; shaped well when fifth at Killarney on debut; improvement likely; chance

runners.name: Mr Withington
runners.spotlight: Modest handicapper at up to 1m5f on the Flat; best watched after a break on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Modest handicapper at up to 1m5f on the Flat; best watched after a break on hurdles debut

runners.name: Steps In Time
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat handicapper at up to 1m2f for David O'Meara, best run when second on fast ground over 1m2f at Haydock last May; makes stable/hurdles debut after a 307-day break.
runners.comment: Fair Flat handicapper for David O'Meara; makes stable/hurdles debut after 307-day break

runners.name: Scopic
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in a Sedgefield bumper on debut in April (2m1f, good; 25-1); best watched on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Well-beaten 25-1 shot in a Sedgefield bumper and is best watched on hurdles debut

runners.name: Ceol Draiochta
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; runner-up in a Ballinrobe maiden hurdle on debut (2m1f, yielding to soft) in August 2024; chance on that form but hasn't run as well since; bit to prove on stable debut after 298-day break.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; chance on his debut 2nd; not run as well since; bit to prove on stable debut

races.race_name: Elliot Henderson Group Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Wise Eagle
runners.spotlight: Looked better than ever when third in the Morebattle Hurdle here in February and posted a good third on the Flat at Musselburgh last month; must enter calculations in refitted cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Very good third in Morebattle here; good third on Flat since so must enter calculations

runners.name: Kihavah
runners.spotlight: Very useful dual-purpose performer; runner-up in Summer Hurdle at Market Rasen last summer and arrives on the back of a good effort on the Flat at Newmarket three weeks ago; very much one to consider back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose performer; good effort on Flat at Newmarket latest; very much one to consider

runners.name: Impero
runners.spotlight: Took this in good style 12 months ago; lightly raced since but advanced his form after nine months off when second of ten in 2m1f handicap at Killarney 15 days ago; up 4lb here but this Irish challenger rates a player.
runners.comment: Took this 12 months ago; excellent second at Killarney 15 days ago; up 4lb but a player

runners.name: The Kemble Brewery
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo who looked better than ever (in first-time cheekpieces) when readily making all in five-runner handicap at Chepstow (2m) 30 days ago; hiked up 9lb but still not taken lightly with headgear retained.
runners.comment: Readily made all at Chepstow latest; hiked up 9lb but still not taken lightly

runners.name: Newport
runners.spotlight: 1-21 over hurdles, his win coming at Musselburgh in December (1m7f, soft); has continued to run well since, notably when second over 2m2f here and on the Flat last time; one to consider.
runners.comment: 1-21 over hurdles but comes here in decent form (second over 2m2f here last month); chance

runners.name: Heart Above
runners.spotlight: Won twice here in 2025 (2m/2m5f; good/good to soft) but hasn't run up to form on his last three starts, last time over 2m6f; others stronger.
runners.comment: Two wins here last year; hasn't run up to his best on last three starts; bit to prove

runners.name: Georgie's Malt
runners.spotlight: 0-7; sound run when sixth over C&D last time (good) but has a bit to find with a couple of these on that form; may improve with cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Behind a couple of these over C&D last time but may improve with cheekpieces now tried

runners.name: Knacker Trapper
runners.spotlight: Didn't seem to stay 2m7f when below-par at Hexham last time, having run creditably on his two previous starts; could go well but on a long losing run.
runners.comment: Didn't stay 2m7f last time but had run well on his two previous starts; could go well

runners.name: Vanilla Dancer
runners.spotlight: 1-21 over hurdles, her win coming at Catterick in February; creditable efforts on all three runs since, including when second over C&D last month (good); sound run at Perth last time (2m5f, good); chance.
runners.comment: 1-21 over hurdles; three sound runs since winning in February and is one with a chance

runners.name: Helsgetaway
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles; sound run from the front when third to a pair of dead-heaters over C&D last time (good); one with a chance.
runners.comment: 0-8; sound run when third to a pair of dead-heaters over C&D last time; chance on that run

runners.name: Stylish Recruit
runners.spotlight: Ended a run of rather disappointing efforts when scoring at Hexham last month (2m4f, good) and better than he showed last time as his saddle slipped on home turn; interesting.
runners.comment: 2m4f win at Hexham last month; better than last run when his saddle slipped; interesting

runners.name: Theirshegoes
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won for a while, but fair runs with cheekpieces on last two starts (fourth in mares' handicap over C&D on good on first occasion); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Not won for a while but comes here in decent form (C&D 4th two runs back); each-way chance

runners.name: Les's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Won four-runner handicap at Hexham last June (2m4f, good to soft; cheekpieces on for first time); disappointing since; needs first-time blinkers to bring about significant improvement.
runners.comment: Won 4-runner race in cheekpieces last June; disappointing since; now tried in  blinkers

runners.name: Gorvennec
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner in May 2024 (3m, good); mainly disappointing over hurdles, particularly last season when back after wind surgery; visor now tried.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; well below-par after wind op over hurdles last term; visor now tried

runners.name: Travis Wheatley
runners.spotlight: Has done well both runs with cheekpieces, winning off a 9lb lower mark at Newcastle in January (2m1f, good to soft) before creditable third off this mark at Ayr last time (2m4f, soft); chance if as effective on this ground.
runners.comment: Sound runs both outings with cheekpieces; chance if as effective on this ground

runners.name: William Of York
runners.spotlight: Has won three times over hurdles and three times over fences, but was disappointing back over hurdles with a visor (retained) instead of cheekpieces at Hexham last time (2m7f, good; 40-1).
runners.comment: Three chase wins and three hurdles wins; disappointing back hurdling at Hexham last time

runners.name: Chapel Green
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D off a 6lb higher mark in September (good); has not fared quite as well since, although more encouraging run when 13l third of seven over 2m1f here last time (good); step back up in trip will suit.
runners.comment: C&D win off 6lb higher last September; not fared quite as well since but better run latest

runners.name: Looking Splendid
runners.spotlight: Has taken well to fences, winning by 11l here on his chase debut (good) before finishing a clear second (again over C&D) last time; went up 9lb for his win and another 4lb for latest run but is one with a chance.
runners.comment: Has taken well to chasing with two sound efforts over C&D; has gone up 13lb, but a player

runners.name: Diamond Mix
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 1m7f at Hexham last year; sound run over same C&D five days ago when clear second to in-form, well-handicapped rival; has a chance but best efforts at shorter.
runners.comment: Two 1m7f wins at Hexham in 2025; 2nd over same C&D last time; chance, but stamina a worry

runners.name: Royal Deeside
runners.spotlight: Both chase wins were over 2m4f on good ground at Hexham last year, latest in August; close third over same C&D last time and worth considering.
runners.comment: Both wins over 2m4f at Hexham in 2025; close third over same C&D last time; a possible

runners.name: Dunkerque
runners.spotlight: Comes into this in good form having won a novice hurdle over 2m4f on good ground at Newcastle in April and on chase debut at Southwell 13 days ago; still feasibly treated; contender.
runners.comment: 2m4f wins on good on last 2 starts (hurdle/chase); still feasibly treated; contender

runners.name: Moodofthemoment
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle in October and not disgraced when sixth here last time; could go well but others in better form.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle in October and fair sixth here last time; may find others in better form

runners.name: Killybegs Jet Lady
runners.spotlight: Winning Irish pointer; yet to score under rules but she is proving reliable and comes here after a good second of 13 in 3m handicap chase at Hexham 22 days ago; very much one to consider off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Good second at Hexham 22 days ago; very much one to consider off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Maggies Boy
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start over fences at Sedgefield (2m5f) in April but soon struggling when pulled up in 2m4f handicap chase at Hexham 22 days ago; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Winning start over fences at Sedgefield; pulled up at Hexham since; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Play By The Rules
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler for Nigel Hawke in 2023; shaped well for his new yard after 17 months off when fifth in 2m4f handicap chase at Newcastle in March, then brought down early at Hexham (2m4f) 22 days ago; well worth another chance off a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Shaped well for new yard with Newcastle fifth, then brought down early at Hexham; player

runners.name: Defying Gravity
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who advanced his form on his chasing debut (in a first-time hood) when a short-head second of eight in 3m handicap at Perth 11 days ago, looking probable winner until blundered last; up 6lb but merits serious consideration in retained headgear.
runners.comment: Short-head second at Perth latest, blundered last; up 6lb but merits serious consideration

runners.name: Rosa Diaz
runners.spotlight: A winning pointer but she failed to progress in five runs over hurdles for Oliver Greenall and Josh Guerriero, signing off with a remote fourth in 3m Southwell handicap last month; goes chasing for her new yard with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Failed to progress for Oliver Greenall and Josh Guerriero; more needed on yard/chase debut

runners.name: Ladies Well
runners.spotlight: A fair winning Irish hurdler but she's been off the track since posting a respectable eighth of 20 in 3m Down Royal handicap in October; she needs to hit the ground running on her chase debut here.
runners.comment: Off since a fair Down Royal eighth in October; needs to hit ground running on chase debut

runners.name: Double Digits
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last spring; back from a winning spell over fences with a very good second of six in 2m Hexham handicap hurdle 22 days ago; another bold showing is on the cards nudged up 1lb with that form having been well advertised since.
runners.comment: C&D winner; very good second at Hexham latest; another bold showing is on the cards up 1lb

runners.name: Scots Poet
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win for two years at Sedgefield (2m1f) in April and backed it up with a good second of nine in 2m Hexham handicap 15 days ago when set a lot to do; this veteran is not taken lightly eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Won at Sedgefield and backed it up with good Hexham second 15 days ago; not taken lightly

runners.name: Ip Up
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence but she arrives in good nick, ridden aggressively when fifth in 2m Warwick handicap in March; she can make her presence felt after a break.
runners.comment: On a long losing sequence but she arrives in good nick; can make her presence felt

runners.name: Miss Friday Lions
runners.spotlight: Yet to register a victory and she was pulled up in 2m2f handicap here last month; hood goes back on now with lots to prove.
runners.comment: Yet to register a victory and she was pulled up in handicap here last month; hood back on

runners.name: My My My Delilah
runners.spotlight: Showed fair form in bumpers but she's yet to match that in this sphere, pulled up in 2m Hexham handicap 15 days ago; has something to prove now.
runners.comment: Fair form in bumpers but yet to match that in this sphere, pulled up at Hexham 15 days ago

runners.name: Walk On Son
runners.spotlight: Remains winless and he has been well below form in handicaps at Perth (2m4f) and Hexham (2m) this spring since undergoing wind surgery; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Remains winless and has been well below form in handicaps at Perth and Hexham this spring

runners.name: Gozo
runners.spotlight: A fair hurdles winner (also fair on Flat) for Ben Brookhouse; pulled up on his first two runs for current yard at Catterick and here this year though and he also came in last of eight on the Flat for R Mike Smith three weeks ago; very hard to warm to.
runners.comment: A fair hurdles winner for Ben Brookhouse but pulled up on first two runs for current yard

runners.name: Simple Star
runners.spotlight: Not seen over hurdles since scoring at Perth (2m) last July but he comes here fit from the Flat and was also a good second on this card 12 months ago; considered.
runners.comment: Not seen over hurdles since Perth success last July but comes fit from Flat; considered

races.race_name: See You In September Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Susie Q
runners.spotlight: 12-1, hinted at promise when a fading seventh of eight in bumper at Huntingdon in November for Paul Robson; this Magna Grecia filly can take a step forward for her new stable.
runners.comment: Seventh in Huntingdon bumper for Paul Robson; can take a step forward for her new stable

runners.name: You Be Lucky Jean
runners.spotlight: Maxios filly; dam, dual bumper/2m4f hurdle winner, is out of a French 1m2f winner; possibilities on her debut, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Maxios filly; possibilities on her debut, especially if the market vibes are positive

runners.name: Koa Beau
runners.spotlight: Lauro gelding; dam, 2m-2m3f hurdle winner, is out of French 11.5f-1m6f AQPS Flat winning sister to 1m4f Flat and 2m1f-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Lamberline; hooded for his debut and the betting can prove an accurate indicator.
runners.comment: Lauro gelding; hooded for his debut and the betting can prove an accurate indicator

runners.name: Maximus Oxo
runners.spotlight: 35,000euros 3yo; half-brother to three winners, notably fair Kaituna River (point/2m4f hurdle); dam 2m4f/2m5f hurdle/chase winner; easy winner of point bumper last March; has since undergone wind surgery so this Maxios gelding is very much one to consider with tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Easy winner of point bumper last March; has had a wind op; very much one to consider

runners.name: Boy Aaron
runners.spotlight: Affinisea gelding; dam, point winner, is a half-sister to bumper/2m hurdle winner On The Wild Side, out of useful 2m hurdle winner; interesting newcomer for Irish yard with good record in bumpers.
runners.comment: Affinisea gelding; interesting newcomer for Irish yard with good record in bumpers

runners.name: Ebchester
runners.spotlight: Ocovango gelding; twice-raced dam is out of 2m-2m4f hurdle winning sister to useful bumper and 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Ashwell Boy; the market can guide for this debutant.
runners.comment: Ocovango gelding; the market can guide for this debutant

runners.name: Little Mayhem
runners.spotlight: Well held in bumpers at Newcastle and Southwell this spring; she needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Well held in bumpers at Newcastle and Southwell this spring

runners.name: Handmedownastar
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who was placed on first two starts over hurdles, more recently when clear second at Newton Abbot (2m3f, good to soft) last month; unraced on ground quicker than that but has a big role to play here.
runners.comment: Clear second at Newton Abbot last month and has another big role to play here

runners.name: Flash Man
runners.spotlight: Won on the Flat when based in France and got off the mark over hurdles at third attempt for current stable, at Plumpton (2m, good) last month; ran at least as well when second under penalty here (2m1f, good) 18 days ago; leading contender.
runners.comment: Won at Plumpton last month and ran at least as well when second here 18 days ago; player

runners.name: Western George
runners.spotlight: Belied 66-1 odds and made quite promising hurdle debut when keeping-on fifth over C&D (soft) in January but was well beaten when upped to 2m7f at Uttoxeter (good to soft) in March and it's hard to know what to expect from him here.
runners.comment: Quite promising fifth over C&D on hurdle debut but well beaten over 2m7f since

runners.name: Midnight Pass
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with respectable fourth on handicap debut at Taunton (2m3f, good) last month but that form leaves her with a bit to find here and she needs to bounce back quickly from a poor run 12 days ago.
runners.comment: Ran quite well on handicap debut but has a bit to find here and was pulled up last time

runners.name: Grey Gamble
runners.spotlight: Displayed promise in two bumpers in the autumn but was remote when fourth here (2m5f, good to soft) on last month's hurdle debut and needs to take a big step forward today.
runners.comment: Showed some promise in bumpers but was remote when fourth here on hurdling debut

runners.name: Conkwell Dancer
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam unraced sister to useful bumper/2m4f hurdle winner Tikkanbar; has just a fair standard to reach on debut but probably best watched all the same.
runners.comment: Has just a fair standard to reach on debut but probably best watched all the same

runners.name: Thepassingcyclone
runners.spotlight: Safely held in two slow-ground bumpers last season; needs the quicker surface to show him in a much better light on this hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Safely held in two slow-ground bumpers last season; hurdle debut today

runners.name: Snowski
runners.spotlight: A big-priced outsider for both hurdle runs; showed some promise before weakening on home turn over C&D (good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) last month but remains best watched.
runners.comment: Showed a glimmer of promise here last month but remains best watched

runners.name: Shalfa
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer who opened hurdling account at fifth attempt, at Plumpton (2m2f, good) last month; not quite in same form when fourth there (2m, good) a fortnight later but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Plumpton winner last month; not quite in same form on latest outing but still considered

runners.name: Hiero Sport
runners.spotlight: Back in prime form when winning from the front here (2m3f, good) last month and now 2-10 over hurdles; recent 3lb rise certainly doesn't look excessive, and he ought to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Back in prime form when winning here (2m3f) last month and now 2-10 over hurdles

runners.name: Lady Dapple Rose
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles for Harriet Dicken but signed off for her with respectable fifth at Ludlow (2m5f, good) last month, where her margin of defeat was magnified by a penultimate-flight error; drops back in trip for stable debut (same owners); interesting to see what Ben Pauling can do with her.
runners.comment: 0-5 over hurdles but signed off for former yard with decent effort last month; interesting

runners.name: Giddyupadingdong
runners.spotlight: Absent for nearly 800 days after winning bumper debut for another stable in 2023 and did not show much in three maiden/novice hurdles (1m7f-2m) for Neil Mulholland last season; has tongue-tie fitted for this handicap debut, though, and remains unexposed; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; showed very little in maiden/novice hurdles; may iimprove in handicaps

runners.name: Whatcombe
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on both Flat starts for this stable in the autumn and showed nothing more than minor promise in three maiden hurdles this spring; may fair better now handicapping but others have more obvious claims.
