runners.comment: Showed nothing more than minor promise in three maiden hurdles this spring; handicap debut

runners.name: Chartwell Jock
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful on the Flat for Andrew Balding; made inauspicious start to hurdling career last season but his close third on handicap debut at Plumpton (2m, good) five weeks ago was much better, and he may yet transfer more of that Flat ability to hurdling.
runners.comment: Third on last month's handicap hurdle debut; Flat ability suggests he can progress again

runners.name: Jacquetta
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles but posted good effort when keeping-on second here (2m3f, good) this month and has plenty of scope to build on that performance; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: 0-5 over hurdles but posted good effort when keeping-on second here (2m3f) this month

races.race_name: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Gata Ban
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over fences but ran big race in defeat and finished ahead of Friends Don't Ask when second over C&D (good) 18 days ago, looking likely winner until collared on run-in; gets another chance off same mark today; respected.
runners.comment: Ran big race in defeat over C&D 18 days ago and gets another chance off same mark today

runners.name: Unspeakable
runners.spotlight: Battled well for narrow win off today's mark at Hereford (3m1f, good; wore cheekpieces) in the autumn but was given break after disappointing effort in December and again ran poorly when reappearing (without cheekpieces) last month; the cheekpieces are missing again here; stablemate Prince Cleni has much stronger claims.
runners.comment: Ran poorly last month, after a break; stable's Prince Cleni has much stronger claims

runners.name: Hatos
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on final four starts last season, most recently when third of 12 here (2m3f, good) one month ago; remains on a workable mark but has stamina to prove over this much longer trip.
runners.comment: Ended last season with string of good efforts (2m3f-2m4f) but has stamina to prove today

runners.name: Prince Cleni
runners.spotlight: Looked set to finish second before last-fence unseat at Hereford (3m1f, good) in March and bumped into revitalised subsequent winner when second at Fakenham (3m5f, good) last month; went close in 2024 edition of this race on only previous visit to Fontwell; good chance.
runners.comment: Went close in 2024 edition of this race and returns here in good form; good chance

runners.name: Twp Stori
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner on slow ground; safely held on chasing debut in November and well beaten when back over hurdles in February; back from another three-month break with cheekpieces refitted; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Safely held on chasing debut in November and well beaten when back hurdling in February

runners.name: Friends Don't Ask
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner (once over C&D); no longer the force of old but returned from break with two pretty good thirds this month, the latter at Bangor (3m, good); contender if in similar form again.
runners.comment: Not the force of old but this C&D winner returned from a break with two thirds this month

races.race_name: Mercedes-Benz Of Chichester Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Fresh As A Daisy
runners.spotlight: Won Uttoxeter novice (2m4f, good) on hurdling debut a year ago; faced tough task when pulled up on handicap debut in March but was shade disappointing when only fifth of seven in calmer waters at Perth (2m4f, good) last month; remains lightly raced but needs to up her game.
runners.comment: Still lightly raced but managed only fifth of seven at Perth in April; needs to raise game

runners.name: Miss Goldfire
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form with C&D win under Ciaran O'Shea this month and is now 3-15 over hurdles; still on workable mark after recent 5lb rise; obvious contender if in similar mood again.
runners.comment: Bounced back to form with C&D win this month and remains on workable mark after 5lb rise
runners.owner: Harry Fry Racing Syndicate Miss Goldfire

runners.name: Queens Venture
runners.spotlight: Led close home to land quite valuable prize for Dan Skelton at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good to soft) last summer; not at same level on any of three subsequent starts for new stable but ran well for long way when fourth of five at Hereford (3m1f, good) this month, after a break, and may be better suited by this shorter trip.
runners.comment: Ran well for long way at Hereford this month, after a break; could have a say

runners.name: Shutfrontdoor
runners.spotlight: Course bumper winner who was placed on last three of her four hurdling starts, most recently when third here (2m1f, good) ten days ago; moves up in trip today and should have improvement to come in handicaps.
runners.comment: Placed on last three hurdling starts; upped in trip for handicap debut; can improve
runners.owner: J Hinds, A Head & G L Moore

runners.name: Smugglers Haven
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner who showed career-best form when game winner of Cheltenham chase (3m1f, good to soft) off today's mark in October; not seen since first-fence fall in December, though, and may not be ideally suited by this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Game chase winner off this mark in October; not seen since first-fence fall in December

races.race_name: Southcoast Homecare Excellence In Care Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Juggernaut
runners.spotlight: Consistent 7yo who has finished second under James Robottom twice since returned to hurdling in March, more recently at Stratford (3m2f, good) last month; still unexposed over this sort of trip and likely to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Second twice since returned to hurdling in March, latterly over 3m2f; in the mix again

runners.name: Jefe Triunfo
runners.spotlight: Generally disappointing in handicaps since winning 2m maiden just under a year ago but Harry Cobden is an interesting jockey booking for this first venture beyond about 2m5f; worth keeping an eye on in the betting.
runners.comment: Largely disappointing in handicaps but Harry Cobden is booked over this new trip

runners.name: Lelantos
runners.spotlight: Now only 1lb higher than when winning at Huntingdon (3m1f, good to soft; also suited by good) in November but has become inconsistent and was pulled up on latest outing seven weeks ago; not easy to predict.
runners.comment: Won off 1lb lower in November but has become inconsistent and was pulled up last time

runners.name: Cinderello
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to score at Warwick (3m1f, good) three weeks ago and is now 2-7 over hurdles; that looked a modest race and she's 6lb higher here but at least we know she's in good nick.
runners.comment: Went up 6lb for winning modest race three weeks ago but is evidently in good nick

runners.name: Jungle Wood
runners.spotlight: Rallied well to open account at Worcester (2m7f, good) last summer and was placed off today's mark on next outing; not yet fully exposed but absent since disappointing run in October.
runners.comment: 2m7f winner last summer; not fully exposed but absent since disappointing run in October

runners.name: Harley's Pride
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 6yo who displayed significant improvement when winning on handicap debut for David Pipe at Doncaster (3m, good to soft) in February; raced too freely and was disappointing favourite for new stable at Fakenham this month but remains of interest.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite on stable debut (raced freely) but won handicap debut in February

runners.name: Glorious Mist
runners.spotlight: 0-14 over hurdles and absent since pulled up on consecutive outings in the autumn; can't be recommended from 7lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: 0-14 over hurdles and absent since pulled up on consecutive outings in the autumn

races.race_name: A. W. Jeffreys (Southampton) Ltd Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Gorcombe Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Gave very good account when close second over C&D (good to soft) last month and subsequent fifth of 12 over C&D (good) was perfectly respectable but his overall strike-rate is just 1-35 and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Twice ran well over C&D last month but has modest strike-rate; others appeal more

runners.name: Lunar Ocean
runners.spotlight: Made all to open account over C&D (good) in first-time blinkers ten days ago, his 11th start over fences; drew nicely clear of the others with the runner-up that day and was only raised 3lb; could be hard to catch.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D in first-time blinkers ten days ago and only went up 3lb for that

runners.name: Majestic Moment
runners.spotlight: 3-6 over fences; returned from break with two good-ground wins last month, the latter gained in fairly good style over C&D; unseated rider mid-race here recently but has strong claims if all goes to plan this time.
runners.comment: Two wins last month, the latter over C&D; unseated last time; still has strong claims

runners.name: Footloose Man
runners.spotlight: Dual 2m5f course winner in early part of last season; returned from five-month break with low-key run here 18 days ago but is versatile regarding ground and his current mark is workable; can feature if that recent outing brought him on.
runners.comment: Dual course winner last season; a possible if recent reappearance run brought him on

runners.name: Millies Mite

runners.comment: Disappointing in handicap hurdles last season; had wind op before this chasing debut

races.race_name: Land & Power Mares' Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)

runners.name: Silver Whisper
runners.spotlight: Last of seven on last month's debut at Plumpton (good) but was prominent in the betting and had Sean Bowen booked, so presumably she is thought capable of better; has tongue-tie added today.
runners.comment: Last of seven on debut at Plumpton last month but was prominent in the betting that day

runners.name: Sky Blue Ribbon

runners.comment: Second in an Irish point in January; new stable has very respectable bumper strike-rate

runners.name: Prestige Runner

runners.comment: Last of six finishers in a recent point bumper but may fare better here

runners.name: Kentford Legacy
runners.spotlight: 12th foal; half-sister to five winners including Kentford Grey Lady (bumper/useful jumps), Swallowshide (hurdle) and Water Wagtail (chase); dam hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to useful chaser The Land Agent; stable has occasional bumper winners; market helpful.
runners.comment: Half-sister to useful jumper Kentford Grey Lady; stable has occasional bumper winners

runners.name: Glad To Be Alive
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-sister to stable's fair hurdle/chase winner The Dream Goes On; dam 2m hurdle winner, half-sister to bumper/jumps Charlie's Vic; stable has bumper winners; no surprise if she features.
runners.comment: Half-sister to stable's fair hurdle/chase winner The Dream Goes On; could have a say

runners.name: La Dame Ecarlate
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam French 2m3f chase winner, half-sister to smart jumpers Aubusson and Toscana Du Berlais; stable's excellent 27% strike-rate in bumpers makes her of strong interest.
runners.comment: Out of a French chase winner; stable has excellent strike-rate in bumpers

runners.name: Panda Legend
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate but was close up over 7f here three runs back and feasible excuses since, on account of the draw and interference; could go well as 1m doesn't bother him.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but close up over 7f here three runs back and feasible excuses since

runners.name: Perfecto Moments
runners.spotlight: No wins in 18 but has threatened and was only 2l away at Happy Valley last time despite being forced wide on the final bend; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Only 2l away at Happy Valley last time despite being forced wide on the final bend

runners.name: Circuit Victory

runners.comment: Reserve; won a dirt trial last month but well beaten since at sprint trips

runners.comment: Ridden by Zac Purton in his last five races but not today after down-the-field runs

runners.comment: Gets new headgear for this first crack at a Class 5 and Purton is booked; respected

runners.name: Frantanck
runners.spotlight: Way better than this grade in his prime and he's not been running badly (7f/1m), latterly finishing fourth under Nichola Yuen from a wide draw; has another to contend with now but he's not without hope.
runners.comment: Way better than this grade in his prime and he's not been running badly

runners.name: Oriental Surprise

runners.comment: It was on dirt when runner-up last time and that was from a similar draw to today's

runners.name: Harry's Hero
runners.spotlight: Finds winning very hard but placed in three of his five starts on turf this year and entitled to be very competitive off this mark.
runners.comment: Finds winning very hard but placed in three of his five starts on turf this year

runners.name: Casa Legend
runners.spotlight: Reserve; both wins over C&D but has to improve on his recent efforts.
runners.comment: Reserve; both wins over C&D but has to improve on his recent efforts

runners.comment: Hasn't been dropping lucky with the draw and that's changed here

runners.comment: Still a maiden and hasn't been shaping like a winner waiting to happen

runners.owner: The Hon & Mrs Kenneth Lau Ip Keung

runners.comment: Reserve; only one blip in his five-race career and looks a winner in waiting off this mark

runners.comment: Beaten a length into third over 6f here last month and he got behind next time

runners.comment: Has shown he can deal with this mark and returned to form with a recent trial victory

runners.name: Rising Elite

runners.comment: Safely held all six starts (6f-1m; mainly on dirt) and probably destined for a lower grade

runners.comment: Stall 14 is a negative but he's been building up some momentum at this track

runners.comment: Has won a trial but very opposable on what he's shown in five races here (6f/7f)

runners.comment: Not a lot to show for his efforts in Hong Kong and had a few chances now

runners.comment: Won on AW in Britain but hasn't been running like a winner waiting to happen in Hong Kong

runners.name: Let's Have Fun
runners.spotlight: Placed for first time in Hong Kong when keeping on for modest third over C&D last month but his latest run was a backward step and he's drawn wide today.
runners.comment: Kept on for third over C&D last month but latest run was a backward step; drawn wide

runners.name: Origin Of Form
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; unplaced over 6f on all seven Hong Kong starts but won over this longer trip when based in Australia, so might get a bit closer to the target this time.
runners.comment: Unplaced over 6f on all seven Hong Kong starts but this extra furlong ought to suit

runners.name: Super Rubick Kid
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise on the last two of his three 6f starts but needs to take a big step forward to overcome outside drawn on this first attempt at 7f.
runners.comment: Has shown promise over 6f but is drawn on the outside for first 7f attempt

runners.name: Alabama Song
runners.spotlight: Just held on to score over C&D last month but finished last from stall 12 here three weeks ago and has the same tough draw today; needs to kick on again.
runners.comment: Narrow C&D winner last month but finished last here recently and has another wide draw

runners.name: Diamond Sparkle
runners.spotlight: Launched career with two fairly encouraging 6f runs (latter was on dirt) but needs improvement over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: Launched career with two fairly encouraging 6f runs but needs improvement today
runners.owner: Lau So Wah & Donna Ng Siu Ying

runners.name: Matzden
runners.spotlight: 7f winner when based in New Zealand; 0-6 in Hong Kong but kept on to make the frame over 6f on last two outings and seems likely to be suited by this move back up in trip; one to consider.
runners.comment: 7f winner in NZ; back up in trip, having made the frame over 6f on last two HK starts

runners.name: President Pegasus
runners.spotlight: Bettered inauspicious debut when keeping-on fourth over C&D three weeks ago; drawn much wider today but has plenty of scope for further progress and James McDonald is a good booking.
runners.comment: Bettered inauspicious debut when keeping-on fourth over C&D three weeks ago

runners.name: Time To Fire
runners.spotlight: Ran okay over 1m here in February but has looked a shade too high in the weights since narrow win over that trip in October, and the drop back to 7f is probably not ideal on first start for three months.
runners.comment: Seemingly too high in weights since narrow 1m win in October; back from three-month break
runners.owner: Thomas Poon Ho Wing, Li Kui Ching & Poon Chi Keung

runners.name: Pope Cody
runners.spotlight: Consistent in handicaps before creditable sixth in Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) in March; disappointed in race fought out by Enthusium and Aerodynamics here three weeks ago but may not have handled the soft ground; has a fighting chance if back on song; blinkers removed after one use.
runners.comment: Disappointed last time but the ground was soft; has a fighting chance if back on song

runners.name: Seraph Gabriel
runners.spotlight: A shade disappointing when only seventh over C&D last month but was commendable fifth in Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) two starts ago and remains lightly raced in Hong Kong; dangerous to ignore.
runners.comment: Slightly disappointing last time but his Hong Kong Derby fifth in March was a good effort
runners.owner: Marc Chan & Lily Chan Li Lei

runners.name: Enthusium
runners.spotlight: Made low-key start to Hong Kong career in February/March but displayed improvement when placed behind Aerodynamics here (1m2f) last month and led close home to pip that rival three weeks ago; 4lb worse off with him today but ought to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Led close home to pip Aerodynamics over 1m2f here three weeks ago; in the mix again

runners.name: Mighty Strength
runners.spotlight: Ran quite well when fifth behind Enthusium and Aerodynamics here (1m2f) three weeks ago and remains on a good mark but has become hard to predict; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Ran quite well here (1m2f) three weeks ago but has become hard to predict
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Li Ming On, Joyce Li Lok Yan & Henry Li Lok Him

runners.name: The Boom Box
runners.spotlight: Got going too late over 7f here three weeks ago but is now 3lb lower than when winning at Happy Valley (1m) in November and looks well worth another crack at this trip; interesting.
runners.comment: Got going too late over 7f last time but moves back up in trip on a good mark; interesting

runners.name: Aerodynamics
runners.spotlight: Gamely made all over 1m2f here last month, his sixth Hong Kong start, and ran big race in defeat behind Enthusium over that trip here last month; respected in current form.
runners.comment: Game 1m2f course winner last month and ran big race in defeat three weeks ago
runners.owner: Daniel Yeung Ngai & Gwenny Wu Pui Kwan

runners.name: Call Me Magnifique
runners.spotlight: 3-9 when trained in Australia but weakened quickly and was well beaten on recent Hong Kong debut (1m).
runners.comment: 3-9 when trained in Australia but weakened quickly and was well beaten on Hong Kong debut
runners.owner: 25/26 Danny Shum Chap Shing Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: The Golden Knight
runners.spotlight: Has a point to prove after last month's substandard 1m run but won twice over that trip in March and could plausibly still have a bit more to offer over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: Below form last month but won twice over 1m in March; moves up in trip
runners.owner: Wilson Ngan Ping Shing, Katherine Ngan Mei Sze Et Al

runners.name: Legendary Years
runners.spotlight: Safely held on first three starts for new stable this season (7f-1m) and has now been unplaced all six Hong Kong starts; probably needs a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Safely held on first three starts for new stable this season (7f-1m); needs drop in grade

runners.name: Joy Of Spring
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in 1m4f Group 3 handicap three weeks ago but placed here (1m/1m1f) on consecutive outings beforehand and Nichola Yuen claims her very useful 10lb today.
runners.comment: Well beaten in 1m4f Group 3 last time; claims if judged on earlier 1m/1m1f handicap form
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Edward Hung & Doris Hung

runners.name: Super Unicorn
runners.spotlight: Now 1lb lower than for the second of his two 1m Happy Valley wins in the autumn but has not been firing on all cylinders lately.
runners.comment: Dual 1m winner at Happy Valley in the autumn but not firing on all cylinders lately

runners.name: Fallon
runners.spotlight: Looked as good as ever when scoring by a neck at Happy Valley (1m1f) in March but this season's record is very mixed and he's on a tough mark now.
runners.comment: On tough mark since win in March and has very mixed record this season

runners.name: Sword Point
runners.spotlight: Hinted at possible return to form when sixth over C&D last month but ought to have been suited by return to dirt when down the field here 18 days ago and has basically become disappointing.
runners.comment: Penultimate run hinted at possible return to form; well held since, though

runners.name: Lo Rider
runners.spotlight: Began this season with 1m course win off today's mark in September but has not been at quite the same level since; hood removed but blinkers remain; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Won off this mark in October but has not been at quite the same level since

runners.name: Romantic Warrior
runners.spotlight: Outstanding 8yo who created history when winning a fourth consecutive Hong Kong Cup over 1m2f in December and has collected another three Group 1s since (1m-1m2f), taking tally at this level to 14; beaten at skinny odds on only previous attempt at 1m4f (in this race in 2023) but still commands huge respect.
runners.comment: Outstanding 8yo with 14 Group 1 wins on his CV; seemingly as good as ever this season

runners.name: Cap Ferrat
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of last season's Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) and subsequently placed in this race; not much has gone to plan this term but he was seemingly back on track when close second in 1m Group 1 here four weeks ago, and this move back up in trip is sure to suit.
runners.comment: Last season's HK Derby winner; seemingly back on track when second in 1m Group 1 in April

runners.name: Numbers
runners.spotlight: Made all in Group 3 handicap here (1m1f) in February, his third Hong Kong start, and bettered that form when second in Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) in March; will also be suited by today's trip but weakened into seventh in 1m2f Group 1 won by Romantic Warrior last month.
runners.comment: Second in HK Derby in March but weakened into seventh behind Romantic Warrior last time
runners.owner: 23/24 Frankie Lor Fu Chuen Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Ka Ying Generation
runners.spotlight: Outran 56-1 odds when third behind Romantic Warrior in 1m2f Group 2 in November and was placed in 1m1f Class 2 handicaps here on last two outings but another minor role is probably his best hope on these terms.
runners.comment: Placed in Class 2 handicaps on last two starts; another minor role probably his best hope

runners.name: Deep Monster
runners.spotlight: Japanese raider who comes here after digging deep to win 1m4f Group 2 in Qatar in February; takes on a very tough opponent but has good each-way claims.
runners.comment: Japanese raider who battled well to win 1m4f Group 2 in Qatar in February; good e-w claims

runners.name: Winning Wing
runners.spotlight: Three course wins over 1m2f this season and also ran well when third behind Romantic Thor and Gentleman Legacy in C&D Group 3 handicap three weeks ago but needs to find hidden reserves to pose a threat at these weights.
runners.comment: Placed behind Romantic Thor in C&D Group 3 handicap this month; much more needed today

runners.name: Gentlemen Legacy
runners.spotlight: Improving 5yo who won consecutive 1m2f races here in February/March and took another step forward when second to Romantic Thor in C&D Group 3 handicap three weeks ago; has a lot more on his plate today, though.
runners.comment: Further progress when second in C&D Group 3 this month; needs to kick on again, though

runners.name: Romantic Thor
runners.spotlight: Evidently well suited by step up to 1m4f when making all from out of the weights in C&D Group 3 handicap three weeks ago; reunited with Zac Purton today but faces a much tougher task on these terms and connections have much stronger claims with Romantic Warrior.
runners.comment: Made all from out of the weights in recent C&D Group 3 handicap; this is much tougher
runners.owner: Peter Lau Pak Fai & Suki Tang Xianfang

runners.name: Rousham Park
runners.spotlight: Gave good account when third of 15 in 1m4f Group 2 in native Japan in March but was no threat to Romantic Warrior when fifth here (1m2f) on latest visit to Hong Kong in December.
runners.comment: Placed in 1m4f Group 2 in Japan in March but well held by Romantic Warrior on earlier form

runners.name: Juneau Pride
runners.spotlight: Consistent in C&D handicaps this year (placed three times) and wasn't disgraced in Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) two starts ago; current mark is workable; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Consistent in C&D handicaps this year and current mark is workable

runners.name: Healthy Happy
runners.spotlight: Game front-runner who won off 1lb higher over C&D in October and was beaten under 1l when fourth to Mugen here last month; well drawn today and Nichola Yuen's 10lb claim makes him of strong interest.
runners.comment: Game front-runner; well drawn to attack under good 10lb claimer Nichola Yuen
runners.owner: Sin Tung Hing, Sin Yuk Lok & Sin Yuk San

runners.name: Mighty Masts
runners.spotlight: Came good in Hong Kong at fifth attempt, with emphatic C&D win last month, and was placed in this higher grade three weeks later; may still have a bigger run in him; one to consider.
runners.comment: Emphatic C&D winner last month and placed in this higher grade three weeks later; player
runners.owner: 24/25 Chris So Wai Yin Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Steps Ahead
runners.spotlight: Consistent over 7f/1m in early part of this season but not at same level since returning from break in March, most recently when down the field on dirt debut; might be happier back on turf but still has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Consistent in early part of season but not at same level since returning from break
runners.owner: Yeung Sing Tung, Sunny Yeung Wah Hon & Janice Yeung Wah Kei

runners.name: Galactic Voyage
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner from six turf starts during this first Hong Kong season; well beaten on recent dirt debut but quickly switches back to turf today, and has possibilities if the new trip suit.
runners.comment: Well beaten on recent dirt debut but 2-6 on over 6f on turf in HK; tries a new trip today
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Lam Ngai And Emily Lam Huen

runners.name: Six Pack
runners.spotlight: In the frame five consecutive times since narrow C&D win in January, most recently when second off this career-high mark four weeks ago; not quite so well drawn today but still very lightly to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: In the frame five consecutive times since narrow C&D win in January; respected

runners.name: Infinite Resolve
runners.spotlight: Excelled himself when second in Classic Mile here in February but subsequent 7f/1m handicap form has been underwhelming and he has a bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Excellent second in Classic Mile in February but subsequent handicap form underwhelming

runners.name: Gorgeous Win
runners.spotlight: Gained this season's win over 6f on dirt; also suited by turf but recent efforts have been disappointing; not the most obvious answer.
runners.comment: 6f dirt winner this season; also suited by turf but recent efforts have been disappointing

runners.name: We Are Hero
runners.spotlight: Gained all six Hong Kong wins over 5f here, the latest a dead-heat in March; well beaten at triple-digit odds when upped in grade and tried over 7f last month; remains opposable over this trip.
runners.comment: Gained all six HK wins over 5f; well beaten at big odds when tried over 7f in April

runners.name: Mugen
runners.spotlight: Returned to form and caused 43-1 shock over C&D last month month, leading close home, but was soundly beaten here three weeks later and it's hard to tell whether this will be a going day.
runners.comment: Surprise C&D winner last month but well held since; too hard to predict

runners.name: Beauty Bolt
runners.spotlight: Progressive over 1m this season, and his 1m2f Hong Kong Derby defeat in March can probably be overlooked; not sure to benefit from this drop back to 7f but reunited with Zac Purton and enters calculations.
runners.comment: Progressive over 1m this season; reunited with Zac Purton for this drop back to 7f
runners.owner: Eleanor Kwok Law Kwai Chun & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen

runners.name: Almighty Lightning
runners.spotlight: Created good impression when winning over 5f here on debut in February and ran well in defeat over that trip on next outing; well beaten over C&D when upped in trip/grade three weeks ago but that was on soft ground and he remains of interest.
runners.comment: Well beaten on soft ground this month but made bright start to career; remains of interest

runners.name: My Mars
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 3yo; led close home to make winning Hong Kong debut over C&D last month and lost out only by a neck here three weeks ago; probably still has more to offer; must be considered.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D on last month's Hong Kong debut and went close here three weeks ago

runners.name: Gustosisimo
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in four consecutive Class 2 races in January-March (6f-7f) but form has dipped lately and his wide draw is another negative.
runners.comment: Placed in higher-grade company this year but form has dipped lately and he's drawn wide

runners.name: Mickley
runners.spotlight: Unable to land a telling blow when dropped to 5f a fortnight ago but has twice finished second over C&D this year and now moves back up in trip with Zac Purton booked; shortlisted.
runners.comment: 5f didn't really suit last time; player if judged on two C&D seconds in February/March

runners.name: Comet Majesty
runners.spotlight: 2-5 as a 2yo in Ireland; absent since respectable seventh in huge-field 7f sales race in September; probably best watched on Hong Kong debut.
runners.comment: 2-5 as a 2yo in Ireland; makes HK debut after 239-day break; probably best watched

runners.name: Elite Golf
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who scored over C&D six weeks ago (first-time hood) and is now 2-4; this rise in grade asks a question of him but he probably still has more in the locker.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last month and is now 2-4; up in grade today; respected

runners.name: Flying Fortress
runners.spotlight: Best known as a miler; not disgraced when dropped back to 6f in first-time cheekpieces at Happy Valley last month but has another wide draw here; others preferred.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when dropped back to 6f last month but has another wide draw here
runners.owner: 23/24 Cody Mo Wai Kit Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Star Rise
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for third over C&D on latest outing in March and has now made the frame on four of his five Hong Kong starts; drawn well again today; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Made the frame on four of his five Hong Kong starts; probably won't be far away

runners.name: Lunar Dash
runners.spotlight: Won over this trip at Happy Valley for Mark Newnham in early part of this season and remains lightly raced but ran poorly when upped to 7f for stable debut in February and has questions to answer on this first start since.
runners.comment: 6f winner in early part of season but ran poorly over 7f on stable debut in February

runners.name: Sugar Sugar
runners.spotlight: Placed in this higher grade since Class 4 win at Happy Valley in December but needs to better his recent efforts; not the most obvious answer.
runners.comment: Placed in this higher grade since Class 4 win in December but not at best lately

runners.name: Embrace Aberdeen
runners.spotlight: Reserve; not yet exposed after only six starts but has been safely held twice in this grade since his Class 4 win in February and needs to take another step forward.
runners.comment: Not yet fully exposed but safely held on both starts since upped to this grade

runners.name: Vigor Happiness
runners.spotlight: Signed off in New Zealand with 7f maiden win in September, his fifth start; drops back in trip for Hong Kong debut; probably one for another day.
runners.comment: Signed off in New Zealand with 7f maiden win in September; back in trip for HK debut

runners.name: Cool Boy
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo who won three in a row over C&D in December-February; had to settle for fourth off today's mark here on last two appearances and his poor run of luck on the draw front has continued but, nevertheless, he features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Enduring poor run of luck draw-wise but this progressive 3yo still commands respect
runners.owner: Lam Ka Lai & Yeung Wing Shut

runners.name: Dashing Basics
runners.spotlight: Reserve; outpaced in rear and always behind on Hong Kong debut at Happy Valley (5f) in March.
runners.comment: Outpaced in rear and always behind on Hong Kong debut at Happy Valley (5f) in March

runners.name: Endurance Express
runners.spotlight: Made winning debut in 5f maiden handicap in Australia in December; not seen since and not easy to weigh up before this Hong Kong debut; market may guide.
runners.comment: Made winning debut in 5f maiden handicap in Australia in December; not seen since

runners.name: Titan Pegasus
runners.spotlight: 2-3 as a 2yo in Australia, the latter win gained in 5.5f Group 3; makes Hong Kong debut after 456-day absence, though, and is entitled to benefit from this outing.
runners.comment: 2yo Group 3 winner in Australia; makes Hong Kong debut after long absence

runners.name: Baby Sakura
runners.spotlight: Coped well with the soft ground when winning comfortably over C&D three weeks ago and is now 2-4; drawn on the inside today and makes obvious appeal.
runners.comment: Won comfortably over C&D this month and is now 2-4; makes obvious appeal
runners.owner: Chan Tak Wa & Ip Wai Yuen

runners.name: King Of Fighters
runners.spotlight: Left disappointing stable debut behind when close second over 6f at Happy Valley in March but two lesser efforts have followed (6f/7f) and he now has it all to prove again.
runners.comment: Placed over 6f in March but two lesser efforts followed; now has it all to prove again

runners.name: Victor The Rapid
runners.spotlight: Scored over 6f in Class 4 at Happy Valley in February and has held his form well since; will be fine over today's longer trip but this move up in grade demands more from him.
runners.comment: Has held form well since 6f Class 4 win in February but moves up in grade today

runners.name: California Waves
runners.spotlight: Placed from wide stall over C&D in March and again ran well when fifth here last month; continues to edge down the weights; considered each-way, despite another awkward draw.
runners.comment: Ran quite well when fifth here last month and continues to edge down the weights

runners.name: Prime Success
runners.spotlight: 2-3 as a 2yo in Britain, signing off with 7f nursery handicap win in September; looks an interesting recruit to the Hong Kong scene but is entitled to benefit from this outing.
runners.comment: 2-3 as a 2yo in Britain, signing off with 7f nursery handicap win in September; HK debut

runners.name: The Red Hare
runners.spotlight: Placed four times since narrow C&D win December, and last month's down-the-field finish is not viewed too harshly (endured wide trip); better drawn today and considered.
runners.comment: Placed four times since narrow C&D win December; latest run best overlooked; considered
runners.owner: Lo Yu Sai, Jasmine Lo Siu Yu Et Al

runners.name: Yee Cheong Glory
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner from first ten starts; just a respectable fifth behind Baby Sakura over C&D (soft) this month and drawn wide again but could still have untapped potential over this trip on a sound surface.
runners.comment: Just a respectable fifth over C&D (soft) this month but may still have untapped potential
runners.owner: Choi Hiu Fung & Choi Yu So Fong

runners.name: Glittering Legend
runners.spotlight: Reserve; evidently well suited by switch to dirt when going close in first-time cheekpieces over 1m here 18 days ago, his fifth Hong Kong start, but this drop back to 7f may not be ideal on his turf return.
runners.comment: Went close on recent dirt debut (1m); this drop back in trip may not be ideal, though

runners.name: Charming Legend
runners.spotlight: Regressive 5yo who finished well down the field over C&D on last two outings and is now 0-4 for new stable; blinkers removed after one use; drawn wide; revival needed.
runners.comment: Regressive 5yo who finished well down the field over C&D on last two outings
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Boniface Ho Ka Kui

runners.name: Class
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; a down-the-field outsider on first three Hong Kong starts (7f-1m).
runners.comment: A down-the-field outsider on first three Hong Kong starts (7f-1m)
runners.owner: Jessica Man Yi Lee & Gregory Chi Ho Ho

runners.name: Golden Champ
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner when based in New Zealand but launched Hong Kong career with four low-key runs (6f-1m); difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Launched Hong Kong career with four low-key runs (6f-1m); difficult to enthuse over

runners.name: Fit For Beauty
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who has collected three course wins (7f-1m) this season and did well to finish third over C&D last month, having raced on outside of the field throughout; drawn wide again but still commands respect.
runners.comment: Progressive 4yo who did well to finish third over C&D last month (raced wide); big player

runners.name: Akashvani
runners.spotlight: Dominted from the front over C&D in January but his subsequent form has been underwhelming and he's still 7lb above that last winning mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner in January but subsequent form has been underwhelming

runners.name: Without Compare
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on first four Hong Kong starts (7f-1m) before holding on gamely to score under enterprising ride here (1m) in March; drops back in trip with James McDonald booked today; could still have more to offer.
runners.comment: Made very solid start to HK career before holding on gamely for 1m win in March; respected

runners.name: Continent Express
runners.spotlight: Ended three-race career in New Zealand with 7f win in September; has the services of High Bowman for this Hong Kong debut but, from stall 14, he's probably best watched.
runners.comment: Ended three-race career in New Zealand with 7f win in September; drawn wide on HK debut

runners.name: Wiki
runners.spotlight: Debut winner at Saint-Cloud in March and bettered that form in Listed event (holds Zaramix) on good ground at Chantilly last time; this French Derby entry is open to improvement and he's a player in this company.
runners.comment: Smart form on both starts and no reason why he won't be competitive in this company

runners.name: Chamali
runners.spotlight: Arc entry; AW winner on his debut in December and stepped up considerably on that form when beating subsequent Listed winner Rumoriste in conditions event over C&D last month; likely to run well.
runners.comment: Arc entry; won both starts and type to hold his own in stronger company; big chance

runners.name: Varandir
runners.spotlight: Arc entry; won at this venue on his debut and followed up over 1m4f at Lyon Parilly; looks ready for the step up in grade and looks a leading player.
runners.comment: Arc entry who has won both starts; ready for a step up in grade and has strong claims

runners.name: Jabracadabra
runners.spotlight: Has won all three starts since being gelded, including a handicap and conditions event on his last two starts; he'll likely have to raise his game further if he's to extend the winning run.
runners.comment: Has won all three starts since being gelded but will have to step up again to win this

runners.name: Baroud
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on previous efforts when winning 1m4f maiden at Saint-Cloud last month; trainer has won this a record seven times but this one will have to raise game to add to that tally.
runners.comment: Won 1m4f maiden at Saint-Cloud last time but more needed to follow up in this company

runners.name: Zaramix
runners.spotlight: Useful performer who has been placed in Group 3 and Listed company on last two starts; however, will need a further step forward if he's to get back to winning ways.
runners.comment: Placed in Group 3 and Listed company on last two starts but more needed to win this

runners.name: Aventure
runners.spotlight: Top-class mare who won this last year (as 2-5 favourite) before her standout moment in landing the Group 1 Prix Vermeille over 1m4f here last September; made a decent start to this season in the Prix Ganay when not getting the clearest of runs, so sets a high standard to surpass on these terms; seems to handle most ground.
runners.comment: Obvious claims here on these terms considering she's won a Group 1; won this last year

runners.name: Sunly
runners.spotlight: Only defeat came in the 1m4f Group 2 Prix de Malleret here in 2025 but looked good either side of that on various ground, including when showing some pace last time to land a Group 3 at Saint-Cloud, when Light The Ghost was third; needs to step up again but has lots of scope to do so; Colin Keane back aboard.
runners.comment: Only beaten once in five starts; looks a top prospect given her scope

runners.name: Santagada
runners.spotlight: Multiple Group 3 winner from 7f to 1m2f in Germany on ground ranging from good through to soft; maintained her progression when landing a Listed event over 1m2f on good going at the start of the month, so couldn't be in better form; Cristian Demuro rides for the first time.
runners.comment: Multiple Group 3 winner from 7f to 1m2f in Germany; Cristian Demuro a new jockey

runners.name: Light The Ghost
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner at Saint-Cloud (1m2f110yds, very soft) last October and not far off that level when third at the same track on her seasonal return behind the comfortable winner Sunly on a quicker surface; holds solid top-three claims but needs plenty more to win in this company.
runners.comment: Third behind Sunly on her recent to action; solid top-three claims
runners.owner: Crispin De Moubray & M L P Consultancy

runners.name: Euboa
runners.spotlight: Won twice from 22 starts, with both of those wins coming in 2023; best performance in Pattern company was when runner-up in a Toulouse Group 3 in 2024, although posted a better figure when third in the Prix Allez France in 2025 at the same level; last two runs don't inspire much confidence and this a tough task.
runners.comment: Not won since 2023 and others should have too much class for her at this level

runners.name: Modern Light
runners.spotlight: Done well in handicaps on her last two starts, but this is her first venture into Group races, and she makes very limited appeal.
runners.comment: Impossible task judged on her RPRs; first run in Group company

runners.name: We'll Defend
runners.spotlight: Always ridden by Christophe Soumillon and in this headgear; won two of her three starts and beat a couple of talented fillies in a C&D Listed event last time; hard to know what to expect against two classy types tried in a Group race for the first time but little doubt she is a nice prospect with the potential for a lot better.
runners.comment: Needs to step up but beat two decent types last time and loads of scope for better

runners.name: Seagulls Eleven
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four
